Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"367: static CopyHeaderChoice\\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\\n369: {\\n370: \\t/*\\n371: \\t * If no parameter value given, assume \\\"true\\\" is meant.\\n372: \\t */\\n373: \\tif (def->arg == NULL)\\n374: \\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n375: \\n376: \\t/*\\n377: \\t * Allow 0, 1, \\\"true\\\", \\\"false\\\", \\\"on\\\", \\\"off\\\", or \\\"match\\\".\\n378: \\t */\\n379: \\tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\\n380: \\t{\\n381: \\t\\tcase T_Integer:\\n382: \\t\\t\\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\\n383: \\t\\t\\t{\\n384: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n385: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n388: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n389: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* otherwise, error out below */\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n391: \\t\\t\\t}\\n392: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n393: \\t\\tdefault:\\n394: \\t\\t\\t{\\n395: \\t\\t\\t\\tchar\\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n398: \\t\\t\\t\\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copy.c%3FL367-400\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "367: static CopyHeaderChoice\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\n369: {\n370: \t/*\n371: \t * If no parameter value given, assume \"true\" is meant.\n372: \t */\n373: \tif (def->arg == NULL)\n374: \t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n375: \n376: \t/*\n377: \t * Allow 0, 1, \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", or \"match\".\n378: \t */\n379: \tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\n380: \t{\n381: \t\tcase T_Integer:\n382: \t\t\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\n383: \t\t\t{\n384: \t\t\t\tcase 0:\n385: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n386: \t\t\t\tcase 1:\n387: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n388: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n389: \t\t\t\t\t/* otherwise, error out below */\n390: \t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n391: \t\t\t}\n392: \t\t\tbreak;\n393: \t\tdefault:\n394: \t\t\t{\n395: \t\t\t\tchar\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\n396: \n397: \t\t\t\t/*\n398: \t\t\t\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\n399: \t\t\t\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\n400: \t\t\t\t */\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copy.c%3FL367-400"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"367: static CopyHeaderChoice\\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\\n369: {\\n370: \\t/*\\n371: \\t * If no parameter value given, assume \\\"true\\\" is meant.\\n372: \\t */\\n373: \\tif (def->arg == NULL)\\n374: \\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n375: \\n376: \\t/*\\n377: \\t * Allow 0, 1, \\\"true\\\", \\\"false\\\", \\\"on\\\", \\\"off\\\", or \\\"match\\\".\\n378: \\t */\\n379: \\tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\\n380: \\t{\\n381: \\t\\tcase T_Integer:\\n382: \\t\\t\\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\\n383: \\t\\t\\t{\\n384: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n385: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n388: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n389: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* otherwise, error out below */\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n391: \\t\\t\\t}\\n392: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n393: \\t\\tdefault:\\n394: \\t\\t\\t{\\n395: \\t\\t\\t\\tchar\\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n398: \\t\\t\\t\\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n401: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"true\\\") == 0)\\n402: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n403: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"false\\\") == 0)\\n404: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n405: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"on\\\") == 0)\\n406: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"off\\\") == 0)\\n408: TO\\\",\\n415: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsval)));\\n416: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_MATCH;\\n417: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n418: \\t\\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tereport(ERROR,\\n422: \\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\\n423: \\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"%s requires a Boolean value or \\\\\\\"match\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdef->defname)));\\n425: \\t * Allow \\\"stop\\\", or \\\"ignore\\\" values.\\n446: \\t */\\n447: \\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"stop\\\") == 0)\\n448: \\t\\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP;\\n449: \\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copy.c%3FL367-450\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "367: static CopyHeaderChoice\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\n369: {\n370: \t/*\n371: \t * If no parameter value given, assume \"true\" is meant.\n372: \t */\n373: \tif (def->arg == NULL)\n374: \t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n375: \n376: \t/*\n377: \t * Allow 0, 1, \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", or \"match\".\n378: \t */\n379: \tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\n380: \t{\n381: \t\tcase T_Integer:\n382: \t\t\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\n383: \t\t\t{\n384: \t\t\t\tcase 0:\n385: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n386: \t\t\t\tcase 1:\n387: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n388: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n389: \t\t\t\t\t/* otherwise, error out below */\n390: \t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n391: \t\t\t}\n392: \t\t\tbreak;\n393: \t\tdefault:\n394: \t\t\t{\n395: \t\t\t\tchar\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\n396: \n397: \t\t\t\t/*\n398: \t\t\t\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\n399: \t\t\t\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\n400: \t\t\t\t */\n401: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"true\") == 0)\n402: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n403: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"false\") == 0)\n404: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n405: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"on\") == 0)\n406: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n407: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"off\") == 0)\n408: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n409: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"match\") == 0)\n410: \t\t\t\t{\n411: \t\t\t\t\tif (!is_from)\n412: \t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n413: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n414: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use \\\"%s\\\" with HEADER in COPY TO\",\n415: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tsval)));\n416: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_MATCH;\n417: \t\t\t\t}\n418: \t\t\t}\n419: \t\t\tbreak;\n420: \t}\n421: \tereport(ERROR,\n422: \t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n423: \t\t\t errmsg(\"%s requires a Boolean value or \\\"match\\\"\",\n424: \t\t\t\t\tdef->defname)));\n425: \treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\t/* keep compiler quiet */\n426: }\n427: \n428: /*\n429:  * Extract a CopyOnErrorChoice value from a DefElem.\n430:  */\n431: static CopyOnErrorChoice\n432: defGetCopyOnErrorChoice(DefElem *def, ParseState *pstate, bool is_from)\n433: {\n434: \tchar\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\n435: \n436: \tif (!is_from)\n437: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n438: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n439: \t\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR,\n440: \t\t second %s is a COPY with direction, e.g. COPY TO */\n441: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s cannot be used with %s\", \"ON_ERROR\", \"COPY TO\"),\n442: \t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\n443: \n444: \t/*\n445: \t * Allow \"stop\", or \"ignore\" values.\n446: \t */\n447: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"stop\") == 0)\n448: \t\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP;\n449: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"ignore\") == 0)\n450: \t\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_IGNORE;\n451: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copy.c%3FL367-450"
  }
}

    "content": "1528: CopyFromState\n1529: BeginCopyFrom(ParseState *pstate,\n1530: \t\t\t  Relation rel,\n1531: \t\t\t  Node *whereClause,\n1532: \t\t\t  const char *filename,\n1533: \t\t\t  bool is_program,\n1534: \t\t\t  copy_data_source_cb data_source_cb,\n1535: \t\t\t  List *attnamelist,\n1536: \t\t\t  List *options)\n1537: {\n1538: \tCopyFromState cstate;\n1539: \tbool\t\tpipe = (filename == NULL);\n1540: \tTupleDesc\ttupDesc;\n1541: \tAttrNumber\tnum_phys_attrs,\n1542: \t\t\t\tnum_defaults;\n1543: \tFmgrInfo   *in_functions;\n1544: \tOid\t\t   *typioparams;\n1545: \tint\t\t   *defmap;\n1546: \tExprState **defexprs;\n1547: \tMemoryContext oldcontext;\n1548: \tbool\t\tvolatile_defexprs;\n1549: \tconst int\tprogress_cols[] = {\n1550: \t\tPROGRESS_COPY_COMMAND,\n1551: \t\tPROGRESS_COPY_TYPE,\n1552: \t\tPROGRESS_COPY_BYTES_TOTAL\n1553: \t};\n1554: \tint64\t\tprogress_vals[] = {\n1555: \t\tPROGRESS_COPY_COMMAND_FROM,\n1556: \t\t0,\n1557: \t\t0\n1558: \t};\n1559: \n1560: \t/* Allocate workspace and zero all fields */\n1561: \tcstate = (CopyFromStateData *) palloc0(sizeof(CopyFromStateData));\n1562: \n1563: \t/*\n1564: \t * We allocate everything used by a cstate in a new memory context. {
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1750: \\t * input function we use depends on text/binary format choice.)\\n1751: \\t */\\n1752: \\tin_functions = (FmgrInfo *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(FmgrInfo));\\n1753: \\ttypioparams = (Oid *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(Oid));\\n1754: \\tdefmap = (int *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(int));\\n1755: \\tdefexprs = (ExprState **) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(ExprState *));\\n1756: \\n1757: \\tfor (int attnum = 1; attnum <= num_phys_attrs; attnum++)\\n1758: \\t{\\n1759: \\t\\tForm_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(tupDesc, attnum - 1);\\n1760: \\n1761: \\t\\t/* We don't need info for dropped attributes */\\n1762: \\t\\tif (att->attisdropped)\\n1763: \\t\\t\\tcontinue;\\n1764: \\n1765: \\t\\t/* Fetch the input function and typioparam info */\\n1766: \\t\\tcstate->routine->CopyFromInFunc(cstate, att->atttypid,\\n1767: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t&in_functions[attnum - 1],\\n1768: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t&typioparams[attnum - 1]);\\n1769: \\n1770: \\t\\t/* Get default info if available */\\n1771: \\t\\tdefexprs[attnum - 1] = NULL;\\n1772: \\n1773: \\t\\t/*\\n1774: \\t\\t * We only need the default values for columns that do not appear in\\n1775: \\t\\t * the column list, unless the DEFAULT option was given. We never need\\n1776: \\t\\t * default values for generated columns.\\n1777: \\t\\t */\\n1778: \\t\\tif ((cstate->opts.default_print != NULL ||\\n1779: \\t\\t\\t !list_member_int(cstate->attnumlist, attnum)) &&\\n1780: \\t\\t\\t!att->attgenerated)\\n1781: \\t\\t{\\n1782: \\t\\t\\tExpr\\t   *defexpr = (Expr *) build_column_default(cstate->rel,\\n1783: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tattnum);\\n1784: \\n1785: \\t\\t\\tif (defexpr != NULL)\\n1786: \\t\\t\\t{\\n1787: \\t\\t\\t\\t/* Run the expression through planner */\\n1788: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefexpr = expression_planner(defexpr);\\n1789: \\n1790: \\t\\t\\t\\t/* Initialize executable expression in copycontext */\\n1791: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefexprs[attnum - 1] = ExecInitExpr(defexpr, NULL);\\n1792: \\n1793: \\t\\t\\t\\t/* if NOT copied from input */\\n1794: \\t\\t\\t\\t/* use default value if one exists */\\n1795: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (!list_member_int(cstate->attnumlist, attnum))\\n1796: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n1797: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdefmap[num_defaults] = attnum - 1;\\n1798: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnum_defaults++;\\n1799: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1800: \\n1801: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n1802: \\t\\t\\t\\t * If a default expression looks at the table being loaded,\\n1803: \\t\\t\\t\\t * then it could give the wrong answer when using\\n1804: \\t\\t\\t\\t * multi-insert. Since database access can be dynamic this is\\n1805: \\t\\t\\t\\t * hard to test for exactly, so we use the much wider test of\\n1806: \\t\\t\\t\\t * whether the default expression is volatile. We allow for\\n1807: \\t\\t\\t\\t * the special case of when the default expression is the\\n1808: \\t\\t\\t\\t * nextval() of a sequence which in this specific case is\\n1809: \\t\\t\\t\\t * known to be safe for use with the multi-insert\\n1810: \\t\\t\\t\\t * optimization. Hence we use this special case function\\n1811: \\t\\t\\t\\t * checker rather than the standard check for\\n1812: \\t\\t\\t\\t * contain_volatile_functions().  Note also that we already\\n1813: \\t\\t\\t\\t * ran the expression through expression_planner().\\n1814: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n1815: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (!volatile_defexprs)\\n1816: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvolatile_defexprs = contain_volatile_functions_not_nextval((Node *) defexpr);\\n1817: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1818: \\t\\t}\\n1819: \\t}\\n1820: \\n1821: \\tcstate->defaults = (bool *) palloc0(tupDesc->natts * sizeof(bool));\\n1822: \\n1823: \\t/* initialize progress */\\n1824: \\tpgstat_progress_start_command(PROGRESS_COMMAND_COPY,\\n1825: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t  cstate->rel ? RelationGetRelid(cstate->rel) : InvalidOid);\\n1826: \\tcstate->bytes_processed = 0;\\n1827: \\n1828: \\t/* We keep those variables in cstate. */\\n1829: \\tcstate->in_functions = in_functions;\\n1830: \\tcstate->typioparams = typioparams;\\n1831: \\tcstate->defmap = defmap;\\n1832: \\tcstate->defexprs = defexprs;\\n1833: \\tcstate->volatile_defexprs = volatile_defexprs;\\n1834: \\tcstate->num_defaults = num_defaults;\\n1835: \\tcstate->is_program = is_program;\\n1836: \\n1837: \\tif (data_source_cb)\\n1838: \\t{\\n1839: \\t\\tprogress_vals[1] = PROGRESS_COPY_TYPE_CALLBACK;\\n1840: \\t\\tcstate->copy_src = COPY_CALLBACK;\\n1841: \\t\\tcstate->data_source_cb = data_source_cb;\\n1842: \\t}\\n1843: \\telse if (pipe)\\n1844: \\t{\\n1845: \\t\\tprogress_vals[1] = PROGRESS_COPY_TYPE_PIPE;\\n1846: \\t\\tAssert(!is_program);\\t/* the grammar does not allow this */\\n1847: \\t\\tif (whereToSendOutput == DestRemote)\\n1848: \\t\\t\\tReceiveCopyBegin(cstate);\\n1849: \\t\\telse\\n1850: \\t\\t\\tcstate->copy_file = stdin;\\n1851: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c%3FL1750-1850\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1750: \t * input function we use depends on text/binary format choice.)\n1751: \t */\n1752: \tin_functions = (FmgrInfo *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(FmgrInfo));\n1753: \ttypioparams = (Oid *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(Oid));\n1754: \tdefmap = (int *) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(int));\n1755: \tdefexprs = (ExprState **) palloc(num_phys_attrs * sizeof(ExprState *));\n1756: \n1757: \tfor (int attnum = 1; attnum <= num_phys_attrs; attnum++)\n1758: \t{\n1759: \t\tForm_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(tupDesc, attnum - 1);\n1760: \n1761: \t\t/* We don't need info for dropped attributes */\n1762: \t\tif (att->attisdropped)\n1763: \t\t\tcontinue;\n1764: \n1765: \t\t/* Fetch the input function and typioparam info */\n1766: \t\tcstate->routine->CopyFromInFunc(cstate, att->atttypid,\n1767: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t&in_functions[attnum - 1],\n1768: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t&typioparams[attnum - 1]);\n1769: \n1770: \t\t/* Get default info if available */\n1771: \t\tdefexprs[attnum - 1] = NULL;\n1772: \n1773: \t\t/*\n1774: \t\t * We only need the default values for columns that do not appear in\n1775: \t\t * the column list, unless the DEFAULT option was given. We never need\n1776: \t\t * default values for generated columns.\n1777: \t\t */\n1778: \t\tif ((cstate->opts.default_print != NULL ||\n1779: \t\t\t !list_member_int(cstate->attnumlist, attnum)) &&\n1780: \t\t\t!att->attgenerated)\n1781: \t\t{\n1782: \t\t\tExpr\t   *defexpr = (Expr *) build_column_default(cstate->rel,\n1783: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tattnum);\n1784: \n1785: \t\t\tif (defexpr != NULL)\n1786: \t\t\t{\n1787: \t\t\t\t/* Run the expression through planner */\n1788: \t\t\t\tdefexpr = expression_planner(defexpr);\n1789: \n1790: \t\t\t\t/* Initialize executable expression in copycontext */\n1791: \t\t\t\tdefexprs[attnum - 1] = ExecInitExpr(defexpr, NULL);\n1792: \n1793: \t\t\t\t/* if NOT copied from input */\n1794: \t\t\t\t/* use default value if one exists */\n1795: \t\t\t\tif (!list_member_int(cstate->attnumlist, attnum))\n1796: \t\t\t\t{\n1797: \t\t\t\t\tdefmap[num_defaults] = attnum - 1;\n1798: \t\t\t\t\tnum_defaults++;\n1799: \t\t\t\t}\n1800: \n1801: \t\t\t\t/*\n1802: \t\t\t\t * If a default expression looks at the table being loaded,\n1803: \t\t\t\t * then it could give the wrong answer when using\n1804: \t\t\t\t * multi-insert. Since database access can be dynamic this is\n1805: \t\t\t\t * hard to test for exactly, so we use the much wider test of\n1806: \t\t\t\t * whether the default expression is volatile. We allow for\n1807: \t\t\t\t * the special case of when the default expression is the\n1808: \t\t\t\t * nextval() of a sequence which in this specific case is\n1809: \t\t\t\t * known to be safe for use with the multi-insert\n1810: \t\t\t\t * optimization. Hence we use this special case function\n1811: \t\t\t\t * checker rather than the standard check for\n1812: \t\t\t\t * contain_volatile_functions().  Note also that we already\n1813: \t\t\t\t * ran the expression through expression_planner().\n1814: \t\t\t\t */\n1815: \t\t\t\tif (!volatile_defexprs)\n1816: \t\t\t\t\tvolatile_defexprs = contain_volatile_functions_not_nextval((Node *) defexpr);\n1817: \t\t\t}\n1818: \t\t}\n1819: \t}\n1820: \n1821: \tcstate->defaults = (bool *) palloc0(tupDesc->natts * sizeof(bool));\n1822: \n1823: \t/* initialize progress */\n1824: \tpgstat_progress_start_command(PROGRESS_COMMAND_COPY,\n1825: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  cstate->rel ? RelationGetRelid(cstate->rel) : InvalidOid);\n1826: \tcstate->bytes_processed = 0;\n1827: \n1828: \t/* We keep those variables in cstate. */\n1829: \tcstate->in_functions = in_functions;\n1830: \tcstate->typioparams = typioparams;\n1831: \tcstate->defmap = defmap;\n1832: \tcstate->defexprs = defexprs;\n1833: \tcstate->volatile_defexprs = volatile_defexprs;\n1834: \tcstate->num_defaults = num_defaults;\n1835: \tcstate->is_program = is_program;\n1836: \n1837: \tif (data_source_cb)\n1838: \t{\n1839: \t\tprogress_vals[1] = PROGRESS_COPY_TYPE_CALLBACK;\n1840: \t\tcstate->copy_src = COPY_CALLBACK;\n1841: \t\tcstate->data_source_cb = data_source_cb;\n1842: \t}\n1843: \telse if (pipe)\n1844: \t{\n1845: \t\tprogress_vals[1] = PROGRESS_COPY_TYPE_PIPE;\n1846: \t\tAssert(!is_program);\t/* the grammar does not allow this */\n1847: \t\tif (whereToSendOutput == DestRemote)\n1848: \t\t\tReceiveCopyBegin(cstate);\n1849: \t\telse\n1850: \t\t\tcstate->copy_file = stdin;\n1851: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c%3FL1750-1850"
  }
}

Otherwise, we can just point input_buf to the\\n176: \\t * same buffer as raw_buf.\\n177: \\t */\\n178: \\tif (cstate->need_transcoding)\\n179: \\t{\\n180: \\t\\tcstate->input_buf = (char *) palloc(INPUT_BUF_SIZE + 1);\\n181: \\t\\tcstate->input_buf_index = cstate->input_buf_len = 0;\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\telse\\n184: \\t\\tcstate->input_buf = cstate->raw_buf;\\n185: \\tcstate->input_reached_eof = false;\\n186: \\n187: \\tinitStringInfo(&cstate->line_buf);\\n188: \\n189: \\t/*\\n190: \\t * Create workspace for CopyReadAttributes results; used by CSV and text\\n191: \\t * format.\\n192: \\t */\\n193: \\tattr_count = list_length(cstate->attnumlist);\\n194: \\tcstate->max_fields = attr_count;\\n195: \\tcstate->raw_fields = (char **) palloc(attr_count * sizeof(char *));\\n196: }\\n197: \\n198: /*\\n199:  * Implementation of the infunc callback for text and CSV formats. Assign\\n200:  * the input function data to the given *finfo.\\n201:  */\\n202: static void\\n203: CopyFromTextLikeInFunc(CopyFromState cstate, Oid atttypid, FmgrInfo *finfo,\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   Oid *typioparam)\\n205: {\\n206: \\tOid\\t\\t\\tfunc_oid;\\n207: \\n208: \\tgetTypeInputInfo(atttypid, &func_oid, typioparam);\\n209: \\tfmgr_info(func_oid, finfo);\\n210: }\\n211: \\n212: /* Implementation of the end callback for text and CSV formats */\\n213: static void\\n214: CopyFromTextLikeEnd(CopyFromState cstate)\\n215: {\\n216: \\t/* nothing to do */\\n217: }\\n218: \\n219: /* Implementation of the start callback for binary format */\\n220: static void\\n221: CopyFromBinaryStart(CopyFromState cstate, TupleDesc tupDesc)\\n222: {\\n223: \\t/* Read and verify binary header */\\n224: \\tReceiveCopyBinaryHeader(cstate);\\n225: }\\n226: \\n227: /*\\n228:  * Implementation of the infunc callback for binary format.   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "167: /* Implementation of the start callback for text and CSV formats */\n168: static void\n169: CopyFromTextLikeStart(CopyFromState cstate, TupleDesc tupDesc)\n170: {\n171: \tAttrNumber\tattr_count;\n172: \n173: \t/*\n174: \t * If encoding conversion is needed, we need another buffer to hold the\n175: \t * converted input data.  Otherwise, we can just point input_buf to the\n176: \t * same buffer as raw_buf.\n177: \t */\n178: \tif (cstate->need_transcoding)\n179: \t{\n180: \t\tcstate->input_buf = (char *) palloc(INPUT_BUF_SIZE + 1);\n181: \t\tcstate->input_buf_index = cstate->input_buf_len = 0;\n182: \t}\n183: \telse\n184: \t\tcstate->input_buf = cstate->raw_buf;\n185: \tcstate->input_reached_eof = false;\n186: \n187: \tinitStringInfo(&cstate->line_buf);\n188: \n189: \t/*\n190: \t * Create workspace for CopyReadAttributes results; used by CSV and text\n191: \t * format.\n192: \t */\n193: \tattr_count = list_length(cstate->attnumlist);\n194: \tcstate->max_fields = attr_count;\n195: \tcstate->raw_fields = (char **) palloc(attr_count * sizeof(char *));\n196: }\n197: \n198: /*\n199:  * Implementation of the infunc callback for text and CSV formats. Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"778: uint64\\n779: CopyFrom(CopyFromState cstate)\\n780: {\\n781: \\tResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo;\\n782: \\tResultRelInfo *target_resultRelInfo;\\n783: \\tResultRelInfo *prevResultRelInfo = NULL;\\n784: \\tEState\\t   *estate = CreateExecutorState(); /* for ExecConstraints() */\\n785: \\tModifyTableState *mtstate;\\n786: \\tExprContext *econtext;\\n787: \\tTupleTableSlot *singleslot = NULL;\\n788: \\tMemoryContext oldcontext = CurrentMemoryContext;\\n789: \\n790: \\tPartitionTupleRouting *proute = NULL;\\n791: \\tErrorContextCallback errcallback;\\n792: \\tCommandId\\tmycid = GetCurrentCommandId(true);\\n793: \\tint\\t\\t\\tti_options = 0; /* start with default options for insert */\\n794: \\tBulkInsertState bistate = NULL;\\n795: \\tCopyInsertMethod insertMethod;\\n796: \\tCopyMultiInsertInfo multiInsertInfo = {0};\\t/* pacify compiler */\\n797: \\tint64\\t\\tprocessed = 0;\\n798: \\tint64\\t\\texcluded = 0;\\n799: \\tbool\\t\\thas_before_insert_row_trig;\\n800: \\tbool\\t\\thas_instead_insert_row_trig;\\n801: \\tbool\\t\\tleafpart_use_multi_insert = false;\\n802: \\n803: \\tAssert(cstate->rel);\\n804: \\tAssert(list_length(cstate->range_table) == 1);\\n805: \\n806: \\tif (cstate->opts.on_error != COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP)\\n807: \\t\\tAssert(cstate->escontext);\\n808: \\n809: \\t/*\\n810: \\t * The target must be a plain, foreign, or partitioned relation, or have\\n811: \\t * an INSTEAD OF INSERT row trigger.  (Currently, such triggers are only\\n812: \\t * allowed on views, so we only hint about them in the view case.)\\n813: \\t */\\n814: \\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&\\n815: \\t\\tcstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE &&\\n816: \\t\\tcstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE &&\\n817: \\t\\t!(cstate->rel->trigdesc &&\\n818: \\t\\t  cstate->rel->trigdesc->trig_insert_instead_row))\\n819: \\t{\\n820: \\t\\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)\\n821: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n822: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\\n823: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot copy to view \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n824: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel)),\\n825: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"To enable copying to a view, provide an INSTEAD OF INSERT trigger.\\\")));\\n826: \\t\\telse if (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_MATVIEW)\\n827: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n828: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\\n829: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot copy to materialized view \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n830: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\\n831: \\t\\telse if (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE)\\n832: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n833: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\\n834: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot copy to sequence \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n835: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\\n836: \\t\\telse\\n837: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n838: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\\n839: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot copy to non-table relation \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n840: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\\n841: \\t}\\n842: \\n843: \\t/*\\n844: \\t * If the target file is new-in-transaction, we assume that checking FSM\\n845: \\t * for free space is a waste of time.  This could possibly be wrong, but\\n846: \\t * it's unlikely.\\n847: \\t */\\n848: \\tif (RELKIND_HAS_STORAGE(cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind) &&\\n849: \\t\\t(cstate->rel->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId ||\\n850: \\t\\t cstate->rel->rd_firstRelfilelocatorSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId))\\n851: \\t\\tti_options |= TABLE_INSERT_SKIP_FSM;\\n852: \\n853: \\t/*\\n854: \\t * Optimize if new relation storage was created in this subxact or one of\\n855: \\t * its committed children and we won't see those rows later as part of an\\n856: \\t * earlier scan or command. The subxact test ensures that if this subxact\\n857: \\t * aborts then the frozen rows won't be visible after xact cleanup.  Note\\n858: \\t * that the stronger test of exactly which subtransaction created it is\\n859: \\t * crucial for correctness of this optimization. The test for an earlier\\n860: \\t * scan or command tolerates false negatives. FREEZE causes other sessions\\n861: \\t * to see rows they would not see under MVCC, and a false negative merely\\n862: \\t * spreads that anomaly to the current session.\\n863: \\t */\\n864: \\tif (cstate->opts.freeze)\\n865: \\t{\\n866: \\t\\t/*\\n867: \\t\\t * We currently disallow COPY FREEZE on partitioned tables.  The\\n868: \\t\\t * reason for this is that we've simply not yet opened the partitions\\n869: \\t\\t * to determine if the optimization can be applied to them.  We could\\n870: \\t\\t * go and open them all here, but doing so may be quite a costly\\n871: \\t\\t * overhead for small copies.  In any case, we may just end up routing\\n872: \\t\\t * tuples to a small number of partitions.  It seems better just to\\n873: \\t\\t * raise an ERROR for partitioned tables.\\n874: \\t\\t */\\n875: \\t\\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)\\n876: \\t\\t{\\n877: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n878: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n879: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE on a partitioned table\\\")));\\n880: \\t\\t}\\n881: \\n882: \\t\\t/* There's currently no support for COPY FREEZE on foreign tables. */\\n883: \\t\\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)\\n884: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n885: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n886: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE on a foreign table\\\")));\\n887: \\n888: \\t\\t/*\\n889: \\t\\t * Tolerate one registration for the benefit of FirstXactSnapshot.\\n890: \\t\\t * Scan-bearing queries generally create at least two registrations,\\n891: \\t\\t * though relying on that is fragile, as is ignoring ActiveSnapshot.\\n892: \\t\\t * Clear CatalogSnapshot to avoid counting its registration.  We'll\\n893: \\t\\t * still detect ongoing catalog scans, each of which separately\\n894: \\t\\t * registers the snapshot it uses.\\n895: \\t\\t */\\n896: \\t\\tInvalidateCatalogSnapshot();\\n897: \\t\\tif (!ThereAreNoPriorRegisteredSnapshots() || !ThereAreNoReadyPortals())\\n898: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n899: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE),\\n900: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE because of prior transaction activity\\\")));\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c%3FL778-900\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "778: uint64\n779: CopyFrom(CopyFromState cstate)\n780: {\n781: \tResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo;\n782: \tResultRelInfo *target_resultRelInfo;\n783: \tResultRelInfo *prevResultRelInfo = NULL;\n784: \tEState\t   *estate = CreateExecutorState(); /* for ExecConstraints() */\n785: \tModifyTableState *mtstate;\n786: \tExprContext *econtext;\n787: \tTupleTableSlot *singleslot = NULL;\n788: \tMemoryContext oldcontext = CurrentMemoryContext;\n789: \n790: \tPartitionTupleRouting *proute = NULL;\n791: \tErrorContextCallback errcallback;\n792: \tCommandId\tmycid = GetCurrentCommandId(true);\n793: \tint\t\t\tti_options = 0; /* start with default options for insert */\n794: \tBulkInsertState bistate = NULL;\n795: \tCopyInsertMethod insertMethod;\n796: \tCopyMultiInsertInfo multiInsertInfo = {0};\t/* pacify compiler */\n797: \tint64\t\tprocessed = 0;\n798: \tint64\t\texcluded = 0;\n799: \tbool\t\thas_before_insert_row_trig;\n800: \tbool\t\thas_instead_insert_row_trig;\n801: \tbool\t\tleafpart_use_multi_insert = false;\n802: \n803: \tAssert(cstate->rel);\n804: \tAssert(list_length(cstate->range_table) == 1);\n805: \n806: \tif (cstate->opts.on_error != COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP)\n807: \t\tAssert(cstate->escontext);\n808: \n809: \t/*\n810: \t * The target must be a plain, foreign, or partitioned relation, or have\n811: \t * an INSTEAD OF INSERT row trigger.  (Currently, such triggers are only\n812: \t * allowed on views, so we only hint about them in the view case.)\n813: \t */\n814: \tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&\n815: \t\tcstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE &&\n816: \t\tcstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE &&\n817: \t\t!(cstate->rel->trigdesc &&\n818: \t\t  cstate->rel->trigdesc->trig_insert_instead_row))\n819: \t{\n820: \t\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)\n821: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n822: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\n823: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot copy to view \\\"%s\\\"\",\n824: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel)),\n825: \t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"To enable copying to a view, provide an INSTEAD OF INSERT trigger.\")));\n826: \t\telse if (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_MATVIEW)\n827: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n828: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\n829: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot copy to materialized view \\\"%s\\\"\",\n830: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\n831: \t\telse if (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE)\n832: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n833: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\n834: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot copy to sequence \\\"%s\\\"\",\n835: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\n836: \t\telse\n837: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n838: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),\n839: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot copy to non-table relation \\\"%s\\\"\",\n840: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tRelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel))));\n841: \t}\n842: \n843: \t/*\n844: \t * If the target file is new-in-transaction, we assume that checking FSM\n845: \t * for free space is a waste of time.  This could possibly be wrong, but\n846: \t * it's unlikely.\n847: \t */\n848: \tif (RELKIND_HAS_STORAGE(cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind) &&\n849: \t\t(cstate->rel->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId ||\n850: \t\t cstate->rel->rd_firstRelfilelocatorSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId))\n851: \t\tti_options |= TABLE_INSERT_SKIP_FSM;\n852: \n853: \t/*\n854: \t * Optimize if new relation storage was created in this subxact or one of\n855: \t * its committed children and we won't see those rows later as part of an\n856: \t * earlier scan or command. The subxact test ensures that if this subxact\n857: \t * aborts then the frozen rows won't be visible after xact cleanup.  Note\n858: \t * that the stronger test of exactly which subtransaction created it is\n859: \t * crucial for correctness of this optimization. The test for an earlier\n860: \t * scan or command tolerates false negatives. FREEZE causes other sessions\n861: \t * to see rows they would not see under MVCC, and a false negative merely\n862: \t * spreads that anomaly to the current session.\n863: \t */\n864: \tif (cstate->opts.freeze)\n865: \t{\n866: \t\t/*\n867: \t\t * We currently disallow COPY FREEZE on partitioned tables.  The\n868: \t\t * reason for this is that we've simply not yet opened the partitions\n869: \t\t * to determine if the optimization can be applied to them.  We could\n870: \t\t * go and open them all here, but doing so may be quite a costly\n871: \t\t * overhead for small copies.  In any case, we may just end up routing\n872: \t\t * tuples to a small number of partitions.  It seems better just to\n873: \t\t * raise an ERROR for partitioned tables.\n874: \t\t */\n875: \t\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)\n876: \t\t{\n877: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n878: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n879: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE on a partitioned table\")));\n880: \t\t}\n881: \n882: \t\t/* There's currently no support for COPY FREEZE on foreign tables. */\n883: \t\tif (cstate->rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)\n884: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n885: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n886: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE on a foreign table\")));\n887: \n888: \t\t/*\n889: \t\t * Tolerate one registration for the benefit of FirstXactSnapshot.\n890: \t\t * Scan-bearing queries generally create at least two registrations,\n891: \t\t * though relying on that is fragile, as is ignoring ActiveSnapshot.\n892: \t\t * Clear CatalogSnapshot to avoid counting its registration.  We'll\n893: \t\t * still detect ongoing catalog scans, each of which separately\n894: \t\t * registers the snapshot it uses.\n895: \t\t */\n896: \t\tInvalidateCatalogSnapshot();\n897: \t\tif (!ThereAreNoPriorRegisteredSnapshots() || !ThereAreNoReadyPortals())\n898: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n899: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE),\n900: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot perform COPY FREEZE because of prior transaction activity\")));\n901: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c%3FL778-900"
  }
}
32. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 1200,
  "path": "src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4",
  "startLine": 1100
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1100: \\t * EACH ROW triggers that we already fire on COPY.\\n1101: \\t */\\n1102: \\tExecBSInsertTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo);\\n1103: \\n1104: \\tecontext = GetPerTupleExprContext(estate);\\n1105: \\n1106: \\t/* Set up callback to identify error line number */\\n1107: \\terrcallback.callback = CopyFromErrorCallback;\\n1108: \\terrcallback.arg = cstate;\\n1109: \\terrcallback.previous = error_context_stack;\\n1110: \\terror_context_stack = &errcallback;\\n1111: \\n1112: \\tfor (;;)\\n1113: \\t{\\n1114: \\t\\tTupleTableSlot *myslot;\\n1115: \\t\\tbool\\t\\tskip_tuple;\\n1116: \\n1117: \\t\\tCHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();\\n1118: \\n1119: \\t\\t/*\\n1120: \\t\\t * Reset the per-tuple exprcontext. We do this after every tuple, to\\n1121: \\t\\t * clean-up after expression evaluations etc.\\n1122: \\t\\t */\\n1123: \\t\\tResetPerTupleExprContext(estate);\\n1124: \\n1125: \\t\\t/* select slot to (initially) load row into */\\n1126: \\t\\tif (insertMethod == CIM_SINGLE || proute)\\n1127: \\t\\t{\\n1128: \\t\\t\\tmyslot = singleslot;\\n1129: \\t\\t\\tAssert(myslot != NULL);\\n1130: \\t\\t}\\n1131: \\t\\telse\\n1132: \\t\\t{\\n1133: \\t\\t\\tAssert(resultRelInfo == target_resultRelInfo);\\n1134: \\t\\t\\tAssert(insertMethod == CIM_MULTI);\\n1135: \\n1136: \\t\\t\\tmyslot = CopyMultiInsertInfoNextFreeSlot(&multiInsertInfo,\\n1137: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t resultRelInfo);\\n1138: \\t\\t}\\n1139: \\n1140: \\t\\t/*\\n1141: \\t\\t * Switch to per-tuple context before calling NextCopyFrom, which does\\n1142: \\t\\t * evaluate default expressions etc. and requires per-tuple context.\\n1143: \\t\\t */\\n1144: \\t\\tMemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));\\n1145: \\n1146: \\t\\tExecClearTuple(myslot);\\n1147: \\n1148: \\t\\t/* Directly store the values/nulls array in the slot */\\n1149: \\t\\tif (!NextCopyFrom(cstate, econtext, myslot->tts_values, myslot->tts_isnull))\\n1150: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n1151: \\n1152: \\t\\tif (cstate->opts.on_error == COPY_ON_ERROR_IGNORE &&\\n1153: \\t\\t\\tcstate->escontext->error_occurred)\\n1154: \\t\\t{\\n1155: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n1156: \\t\\t\\t * Soft error occurred, skip this tuple and just make\\n1157: \\t\\t\\t * ErrorSaveContext ready for the next NextCopyFrom. Since we\\n1158: \\t\\t\\t * don't set details_wanted and error_data is not to be filled,\\n1159: \\t\\t\\t * just resetting error_occurred is enough.\\n1160: \\t\\t\\t */\\n1161: \\t\\t\\tcstate->escontext->error_occurred = false;\\n1162: \\n1163: \\t\\t\\t/* Report that this tuple was skipped by the ON_ERROR clause */\\n1164: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c%3FL1100-1200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1100: \t * EACH ROW triggers that we already fire on COPY.\n1101: \t */\n1102: \tExecBSInsertTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo);\n1103: \n1104: \tecontext = GetPerTupleExprContext(estate);\n1105: \n1106: \t/* Set up callback to identify error line number */\n1107: \terrcallback.callback = CopyFromErrorCallback;\n1108: \terrcallback.arg = cstate;\n1109: \terrcallback.previous = error_context_stack;\n1110: \terror_context_stack = &errcallback;\n1111: \n1112: \tfor (;;)\n1113: \t{\n1114: \t\tTupleTableSlot *myslot;\n1115: \t\tbool\t\tskip_tuple;\n1116: \n1117: \t\tCHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();\n1118: \n1119: \t\t/*\n1120: \t\t * Reset the per-tuple exprcontext. We do this after every tuple, to\n1121: \t\t * clean-up after expression evaluations etc.\n1122: \t\t */\n1123: \t\tResetPerTupleExprContext(estate);\n1124: \n1125: \t\t/* select slot to (initially) load row into */\n1126: \t\tif (insertMethod == CIM_SINGLE || proute)\n1127: \t\t{\n1128: \t\t\tmyslot = singleslot;\n1129: \t\t\tAssert(myslot != NULL);\n1130: \t\t}\n1131: \t\telse\n1132: \t\t{\n1133: \t\t\tAssert(resultRelInfo == target_resultRelInfo);\n1134: \t\t\tAssert(insertMethod == CIM_MULTI);\n1135: \n1136: \t\t\tmyslot = CopyMultiInsertInfoNextFreeSlot(&multiInsertInfo,\n1137: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t resultRelInfo);\n1138: \t\t}\n1139: \n1140: \t\t/*\n1141: \t\t * Switch to per-tuple context before calling NextCopyFrom, which does\n1142: \t\t * evaluate default expressions etc. and requires per-tuple context.\n1143: \t\t */\n1144: \t\tMemoryContextSwitchTo(GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));\n1145: \n1146: \t\tExecClearTuple(myslot);\n1147: \n1148: \t\t/* Directly store the values/nulls array in the slot */\n1149: \t\tif (!NextCopyFrom(cstate, econtext, myslot->tts_values, myslot->tts_isnull))\n1150: \t\t\tbreak;\n1151: \n1152: \t\tif (cstate->opts.on_error == COPY_ON_ERROR_IGNORE &&\n1153: \t\t\tcstate->escontext->error_occurred)\n1154: \t\t{\n1155: \t\t\t/*\n1156: \t\t\t * Soft error occurred, skip this tuple and just make\n1157: \t\t\t * ErrorSaveContext ready for the next NextCopyFrom. Since we\n1158: \t\t\t * don't set details_wanted and error_data is not to be filled,\n1159: \t\t\t * just resetting error_occurred is enough.\n1160: \t\t\t */\n1161: \t\t\tcstate->escontext->error_occurred = false;\n1162: \n1163: \t\t\t/* Report that this tuple was skipped by the ON_ERROR clause */\n1164: \t\t\tpgstat_progress_update_param(PROGRESS_COPY_TUPLES_SKIPPED,\n1165: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t cstate->num_errors);\n1166: \n1167: \t\t\tif (cstate->opts.reject_limit > 0 &&\n1168: \t\t\t\tcstate->num_errors > cstate->opts.reject_limit)\n1169: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n1170: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TEXT_REPRESENTATION),\n1171: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"skipped more than REJECT_LIMIT (%\" PRId64 \") rows due to data type incompatibility\",\n1172: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcstate->opts.reject_limit)));\n1173: \n1174: \t\t\t/* Repeat NextCopyFrom() until no soft error occurs */\n1175: \t\t\tcontinue;\n1176: \t\t}\n1177: \n1178: \t\tExecStoreVirtualTuple(myslot);\n1179: \n1180: \t\t/*\n1181: \t\t * Constraints and where clause might reference the tableoid column,\n1182: \t\t * so (re-)initialize tts_tableOid before evaluating them.\n1183: \t\t */\n1184: \t\tmyslot->tts_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(target_resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc);\n1185: \n1186: \t\t/* Triggers and stuff need to be invoked in query context. */\n1187: \t\tMemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);\n1188: \n1189: \t\tif (cstate->whereClause)\n1190: \t\t{\n1191: \t\t\tecontext->ecxt_scantuple = myslot;\n1192: \t\t\t/* Skip items that don't match COPY's WHERE clause */\n1193: \t\t\tif (!ExecQual(cstate->qualexpr, econtext))\n1194: \t\t\t{\n1195: \t\t\t\t/*\n1196: \t\t\t\t * Report that this tuple was filtered out by the WHERE\n1197: \t\t\t\t * clause.\n1198: \t\t\t\t */\n1199: \t\t\t\tpgstat_progress_update_param(PROGRESS_COPY_TUPLES_EXCLUDED,\n1200: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t ++excluded);\n1201: ",

econtext and have switched into the\\n902: \\t\\t * per-tuple memory context in it.\\n903: \\t\\t */\\n904: \\t\\tAssert(econtext != NULL);\\n905: \\t\\tAssert(CurrentMemoryContext == \\n911: \\treturn true;\\n912: }\\n913: \\n914: /* Implementation of the per-row callback for text format */\\n915: bool\\n916: CopyFromTextOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext, Datum for CSV format */\\n923: bool\\n924: CopyFromCSVOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext, Datum *values,\\n925: \\t\\t\\t\\t  bool *nulls)\\n926: {\\n927: \\treturn pg_attribute_always_inline to reduce function call overhead\\n934:  * and to help compilers to optimize away the 'is_csv' condition.\\n935:  */\\n936: static pg_attribute_always_inline bool\\n937:   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "870: bool\n871: NextCopyFrom(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext,\n872: \t\t\t Datum *values, bool *nulls)\n873: {\n874: \tTupleDesc\ttupDesc;\n875: \tAttrNumber\tnum_phys_attrs,\n876: \t\t\t\tnum_defaults = cstate->num_defaults;\n877: \tint\t\t\ti;\n878: \tint\t\t   *defmap = cstate->defmap;\n879: \tExprState **defexprs = cstate->defexprs;\n880: \n881: \ttupDesc = RelationGetDescr(cstate->rel);\n882: \tnum_phys_attrs = tupDesc->natts;\n883: \n884: \t/* Initialize all values for row to NULL */\n885: \tMemSet(values, 0, num_phys_attrs * sizeof(Datum));\n886: \tMemSet(nulls, true, num_phys_attrs * sizeof(bool));\n887: \tMemSet(cstate->defaults, false, num_phys_attrs * sizeof(bool));\n888: \n889: \t/* Get one row from source */\n890: \tif (!cstate->routine->CopyFromOneRow(cstate, econtext, values, nulls))\n891: \t\treturn false;\n892: \n893: \t/*\n894: \t * Now compute and insert any defaults available for the columns not\n895: \t * provided by the input data.  Anything not processed here or above will\n896: \t * remain NULL.\n897: \t */\n898: \tfor (i = 0; i < num_defaults; i++)\n899: \t{\n900: \t\t/*\n901: \t\t * The caller must supply econtext and have switched into the\n902: \t\t * per-tuple memory context in it.\n903: \t\t */\n904: \t\tAssert(econtext != NULL);\n905: \t\tAssert(CurrentMemoryContext == econtext->ecxt_per_tuple_memory);\n906: \n907: \t\tvalues[defmap[i]] = ExecEvalExpr(defexprs[defmap[i]], econtext,\n908: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t &nulls[defmap[i]]);\n909: \t}\n910: \n911: \treturn true;\n912: }\n913: \n914: /* Implementation of the per-row callback for text format */\n915: bool\n916: CopyFromTextOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext, Datum *values,\n917: \t\t\t\t   bool *nulls)\n918: {\n919: \treturn CopyFromTextLikeOneRow(cstate, econtext, values, nulls, false);\n920: }\n921: \n922: /* Implementation of the per-row callback for CSV format */\n923: bool\n924: CopyFromCSVOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext, Datum *values,\n925: \t\t\t\t  bool *nulls)\n926: {\n927: \treturn CopyFromTextLikeOneRow(cstate, econtext, values, nulls, true);\n928: }\n929: \n930: /*\n931:  * Workhorse for CopyFromTextOneRow() and CopyFromCSVOneRow().\n932:  *\n933:  * We use pg_attribute_always_inline to reduce function call overhead\n934:  * and to help compilers to optimize away the 'is_csv' condition.\n935:  */\n936: static pg_attribute_always_inline bool\n937: CopyFromTextLikeOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext,\n938: \t\t\t\t\t   Datum *values, bool *nulls, bool is_csv)\n939: {\n940: \tTupleDesc\ttupDesc;\n941: \tAttrNumber\tattr_count;\n942: \tFmgrInfo   *in_functions = cstate->in_functions;\n943: \tOid\t\t   *typioparams = cstate->typioparams;\n944: \tExprState **defexprs = cstate->defexprs;\n945: \tchar\t  **field_strings;\n946: \tListCell   *cur;\n947: \tint\t\t\tfldct;\n948: \tint\t\t\tfieldno;\n949: \tchar\t   *string;\n950: \n951: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfromparse.c%3FL870-950"
  }
}

    "content": "935:  */\n936: static pg_attribute_always_inline bool\n937: CopyFromTextLikeOneRow(CopyFromState cstate, ExprContext *econtext,\n938: \t\t\t\t\t   Datum *values, bool *nulls, bool is_csv)\n939: {\n940: \tTupleDesc\ttupDesc;\n941: \tAttrNumber\tattr_count;\n942: \tFmgrInfo   *in_functions = cstate->in_functions;\n943: \tOid\t\t   *typioparams = cstate->typioparams;\n944: \tExprState **defexprs = cstate->defexprs;\n945: \tchar\t  **field_strings;\n946: \tListCell   *cur;\n947: \tint\t\t\tfldct;\n948: \tint\t\t\tfieldno;\n949: \tchar\t   *string;\n950: \n951: \ttupDesc = RelationGetDescr(cstate->rel);\n952: \tattr_count = list_length(cstate->attnumlist);\n953: \n954: \t/* read raw fields in the next line */\n955: \tif (!NextCopyFromRawFieldsInternal(cstate, &field_strings, &fldct, is_csv))\n956: \t\treturn false;\n957: \n958: \t/* check for overflowing fields */\n959: \tif (attr_count > 0 && fldct > attr_count)\n960: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n961: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n962: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"extra data after last expected column\")));\n963: \n964: \tfieldno = 0;\n965: \n966: \t/* Loop to read the user attributes on the line. */\n967: \tforeach(cur, cstate->attnumlist)\n968: \t{\n969: \t\tint\t\t\tattnum = lfirst_int(cur);\n970: \t\tint\t\t\tm = attnum - 1;\n971: \t\tForm_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(tupDesc, m);\n972: \n973: \t\tif (fieldno >= fldct)\n974: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n975: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n976: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"missing data for column \\\"%s\\\"\",\n977: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tNameStr(att->attname))));\n978: \t\tstring = field_strings[fieldno++];\n979: \n980: \t\tif (cstate->convert_select_flags &&\n981: \t\t\t!cstate->convert_select_flags[m])\n982: \t\t{\n983: \t\t\t/* ignore input field, leaving column as NULL */\n984: \t\t\tcontinue;\n985: \t\t}\n986: \n987: \t\tif (is_csv)\n988: \t\t{\n989: \t\t\tif (string == NULL &&\n990: \t\t\t\tcstate->opts.force_notnull_flags[m])\n991: \t\t\t{\n992: \t\t\t\t/*\n993: \t\t\t\t * FORCE_NOT_NULL option is set and column is NULL - convert\n994: \t\t\t\t * it to the NULL string.\n995: \t\t\t\t */\n996: \t\t\t\tstring = cstate->opts.null_print;\n997: \t\t\t}\n998: \t\t\telse if (string != NULL && cstate->opts.force_null_flags[m]\n999: \t\t\t\t\t && strcmp(string, cstate->opts.null_print) == 0)\n1000: \t\t\t{\n1001: \t\t\t\t/*\n1002: \t\t\t\t * FORCE_NULL option is set and column matches the NULL\n1003: \t\t\t\t * string. It must have been quoted, or otherwise the string\n1004: \t\t\t\t * would already have been set to NULL. Convert it to NULL as\n1005: \t\t\t\t * specified.\n1006: \t\t\t\t */\n1007: \t\t\t\tstring = NULL;\n1008: \t\t\t}\n1009: \t\t}\n1010: \n1011: \t\tcstate->cur_attname = NameStr(att->attname);\n1012: \t\tcstate->cur_attval = string;\n1013: \n1014: \t\tif (string != NULL)\n1015: \t\t\tnulls[m] = false;\n1016: \n1017: \t\tif (cstate->defaults[m])\n1018: \t\t{\n1019: \t\t\t/* We must have switched into the per-tuple memory context */\n1020: \t\t\tAssert(econtext != NULL);\n1021: \t\t\tAssert(CurrentMemoryContext == econtext->ecxt_per_tuple_memory);\n1022: \n1023: \t\t\tvalues[m] = ExecEvalExpr(defexprs[m], econtext, &nulls[m]);\n1024: \t\t}\n1025: \n1026: \t\t/*\n1027: \t\t * If ON_ERROR is specified with IGNORE, skip rows with soft errors\n1028: \t\t */\n1029: \t\telse if (!InputFunctionCallSafe(&in_functions[m],\n1030: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tstring,\n1031: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttypioparams[m],\n1032: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tatt->atttypmod,\n1033: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(Node *) cstate->escontext,\n1034: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t&values[m]))\n1035: \t\t{\n1036: \t\t\tAssert(cstate->opts.on_error != COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP);\n1037: \n1038: \t\t\tcstate->num_errors++;\n1039: \n1040: \t\t\tif (cstate->opts.log_verbosity == COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_VERBOSE)\n1041: \t\t\t{\n1042: \t\t\t\t/*\n1043: \t\t\t\t * Since we emit line number and column info in the below\n1044: \t\t\t\t * notice message, we suppress error context information other\n1045: \t\t\t\t * than the relation name.\n1046: \t\t\t\t */\n1047: \t\t\t\tAssert(!cstate->relname_only);\n1048: \t\t\t\tcstate->relname_only = true;\n1049: \n1050: \t\t\t\tif (cstate->cur_attval)\n1051: ",

PqMsg_CopyInResponse);\\n178: \\tpq_sendbyte(&buf, format);\\t/* overall format */\\n179: \\tpq_sendint16(&buf, natts);\\n180: \\tfor (i = 0; i < natts; i++)\\n181: \\t\\tpq_sendint16(&buf, format); /* per-column formats */\\n182: \\tpq_endmessage(&buf);\\n183: \\tcstate->copy_src = COPY_FRONTEND;\\n184: \\tcstate->fe_msgbuf = makeStringInfo();\\n185: \\t/* We *must* flush here to ensure FE knows it can send. */\\n186: \\tpq_flush();\\n187: }\\n188: \\n189: void\\n190: ReceiveCopyBinaryHeader(CopyFromState cstate)\\n191: {\\n192: \\tchar\\t\\treadSig[11];\\n193: \\tint32\\t\\ttmp;\\n194: \\n195: \\t/* Signature */\\n196: \\tif (CopyReadBinaryData(cstate, readSig, 11) != 11 ||\\n197: \\t\\tmemcmp(readSig, BinarySignature, 11) != 0)\\n198: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n200: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY file signature not recognized\\\")));\\n201: \\t/* Flags field */\\n202: \\tif (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp))\\n203: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"invalid COPY file header (missing flags)\\\")));\\n206: \\tif ((tmp & (1 << 16)) != 0)\\n207: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"invalid COPY file header (WITH OIDS)\\\")));\\n210: \\ttmp &= ~(1 << 16);\\n211: \\tif ((tmp >> 16) != 0)\\n212: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n213: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"unrecognized critical flags in COPY file header\\\")));\\n215: \\t/* Header extension length */\\n216: \\tif (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp) ||\\n217: \\t\\ttmp < 0)\\n218: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n219: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n220: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"invalid COPY file header (missing length)\\\")));\\n221: \\t/* Skip extension header, if present */\\n222: \\twhile (tmp-- > 0)\\n223: \\t{\\n224: \\t\\tif (CopyReadBinaryData(cstate, readSig, 1) != 1)\\n225: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n226: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\\n227: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"invalid COPY file header (wrong length)\\\")));\\n228: \\t}\\n229: }\\n230: \\n231: /*\\n232:  * CopyGetData reads data from the source (file or frontend)\\n233:  *\\n234:  * We attempt to read at least minread, and at most maxread, bytes from\\n235:  * the source.  The actual number of bytes read is returned; if this is\\n236:  * less than minread, EOF was detected.\\n237:  *\\n238:  * Note: when copying from the frontend, we expect a proper EOF mark per\\n239:  * protocol; if the frontend simply drops the connection, we raise error.\\n240:  * It seems unwise to allow the COPY IN to complete normally in that case.\\n241:  *\\n242:  * NB: no data conversion is applied here.\\n243:  */\\n244: static int\\n245: CopyGetData(CopyFromState cstate, void *databuf, int minread, int maxread)\\n246: {\\n247: \\tint\\t\\t\\tbytesread = 0;\\n248: \\n249: \\tswitch (cstate->copy_src)\\n250: \\t{\\n251: \\t\\tcase COPY_FILE:\\n252: \\t\\t\\tbytesread = fread(databuf, 1, maxread, cstate->copy_file);\\n253: \\t\\t\\tif (ferror(cstate->copy_file))\\n254: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n255: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode_for_file_access(),\\n256: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"could not read from COPY file: %m\\\")));\\n257: \\t\\t\\tif (bytesread == 0)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t\\tcstate->raw_reached_eof = true;\\n259: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n260: \\t\\tcase COPY_FRONTEND:\\n261: \\t\\t\\twhile (maxread > 0 && bytesread < minread && !cstate->raw_reached_eof)\\n262: \\t\\t\\t{\\n263: \\t\\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tavail;\\n264: \\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\twhile (cstate->fe_msgbuf->cursor >= cstate->fe_msgbuf->len)\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* Try to receive another message */\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tmtype;\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tmaxmsglen;\\n270: \\n271: \\t\\t\\treadmessage:\\n272: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tHOLD_CANCEL_INTERRUPTS();\\n273: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpq_startmsgread();\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmtype = pq_getbyte();\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif (mtype == EOF)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),\\n278: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"unexpected EOF on client connection with an open   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "155: static pg_attribute_always_inline bool NextCopyFromRawFieldsInternal(CopyFromState cstate,\n156: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t char ***fields,\n157: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t int *nfields,\n158: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t bool is_csv);\n159: \n160: \n161: /* Low-level communications functions */\n162: static int\tCopyGetData(CopyFromState cstate, void *databuf,\n163: \t\t\t\t\t\tint minread, int maxread);\n164: static inline bool CopyGetInt32(CopyFromState cstate, int32 *val);\n165: static inline bool CopyGetInt16(CopyFromState cstate, int16 *val);\n166: static void CopyLoadInputBuf(CopyFromState cstate);\n167: static int\tCopyReadBinaryData(CopyFromState cstate, char *dest, int nbytes);\n168: \n169: void\n170: ReceiveCopyBegin(CopyFromState cstate)\n171: {\n172: \tStringInfoData buf;\n173: \tint\t\t\tnatts = list_length(cstate->attnumlist);\n174: \tint16\t\tformat = (cstate->opts.binary ? 1 : 0);\n175: \tint\t\t\ti;\n176: \n177: \tpq_beginmessage(&buf, PqMsg_CopyInResponse);\n178: \tpq_sendbyte(&buf, format);\t/* overall format */\n179: \tpq_sendint16(&buf, natts);\n180: \tfor (i = 0; i < natts; i++)\n181: \t\tpq_sendint16(&buf, format); /* per-column formats */\n182: \tpq_endmessage(&buf);\n183: \tcstate->copy_src = COPY_FRONTEND;\n184: \tcstate->fe_msgbuf = makeStringInfo();\n185: \t/* We *must* flush here to ensure FE knows it can send. */\n186: \tpq_flush();\n187: }\n188: \n189: void\n190: ReceiveCopyBinaryHeader(CopyFromState cstate)\n191: {\n192: \tchar\t\treadSig[11];\n193: \tint32\t\ttmp;\n194: \n195: \t/* Signature */\n196: \tif (CopyReadBinaryData(cstate, readSig, 11) != 11 ||\n197: \t\tmemcmp(readSig, BinarySignature, 11) != 0)\n198: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n199: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n200: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY file signature not recognized\")));\n201: \t/* Flags field */\n202: \tif (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp))\n203: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n204: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n205: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"invalid COPY file header (missing flags)\")));\n206: \tif ((tmp & (1 << 16)) != 0)\n207: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n208: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n209: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"invalid COPY file header (WITH OIDS)\")));\n210: \ttmp &= ~(1 << 16);\n211: \tif ((tmp >> 16) != 0)\n212: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n213: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n214: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"unrecognized critical flags in COPY file header\")));\n215: \t/* Header extension length */\n216: \tif (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp) ||\n217: \t\ttmp < 0)\n218: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n219: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n220: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"invalid COPY file header (missing length)\")));\n221: \t/* Skip extension header, if present */\n222: \twhile (tmp-- > 0)\n223: \t{\n224: \t\tif (CopyReadBinaryData(cstate, readSig, 1) != 1)\n225: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n226: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),\n227: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"invalid COPY file header (wrong length)\")));\n228: \t}\n229: }\n230: \n231: /*\n232:  * CopyGetData reads data from the source (file or frontend)\n233:  *\n234:  * We attempt to read at least minread, and at most maxread, bytes from\n235:  * the source.  The actual number of bytes read is returned; if this is\n236:  * less than minread, EOF was detected.\n237:  *\n238:  * Note: when copying from the frontend, we expect a proper EOF mark per\n239:  * protocol; if the frontend simply drops the connection, we raise error.\n240:  * It seems unwise to allow the COPY IN to complete normally in that case.\n241:  *\n242:  * NB: no data conversion is applied here.\n243:  */\n244: static int\n245: CopyGetData(CopyFromState cstate, void *databuf, int minread, int maxread)\n246: {\n247: \tint\t\t\tbytesread = 0;\n248: \n249: \tswitch (cstate->copy_src)\n250: \t{\n251: \t\tcase COPY_FILE:\n252: \t\t\tbytesread = fread(databuf, 1, maxread, cstate->copy_file);\n253: \t\t\tif (ferror(cstate->copy_file))\n254: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n255: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode_for_file_access(),\n256: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"could not read from COPY file: %m\")));\n257: \t\t\tif (bytesread == 0)\n258: \t\t\t\tcstate->raw_reached_eof = true;\n259: \t\t\tbreak;\n260: \t\tcase COPY_FRONTEND:\n261: \t\t\twhile (maxread > 0 && bytesread < minread && !cstate->raw_reached_eof)\n262: \t\t\t{\n263: \t\t\t\tint\t\t\tavail;\n264: \n265: \t\t\t\twhile (cstate->fe_msgbuf->cursor >= cstate->fe_msgbuf->len)\n266: \t\t\t\t{\n267: \t\t\t\t\t/* Try to receive another message */\n268: \t\t\t\t\tint\t\t\tmtype;\n269: \t\t\t\t\tint\t\t\tmaxmsglen;\n270: \n271: \t\t\treadmessage:\n272: \t\t\t\t\tHOLD_CANCEL_INTERRUPTS();\n273: \t\t\t\t\tpq_startmsgread();\n274: \t\t\t\t\tmtype = pq_getbyte();\n275: \t\t\t\t\tif (mtype == EOF)\n276: \t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n277: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),\n278: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"unexpected EOF on client connection with an open transaction\")));\n279: \t\t\t\t\t/* Validate message type and set packet size limit */\n280: \t\t\t\t\tswitch (mtype)\n281: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/commands/copyfromparse.c%3FL155-280"
  }
}
