  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: --\\n2: -- COPY\\n3: --\\n4: \\n5: -- directory paths are passed to us in environment variables\\n6: \\\\getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR\\n7: \\\\getenv abs_builddir PG_ABS_BUILDDIR\\n8: \\n9: --- test copying in CSV mode with various styles\\n10: --- of embedded line ending characters\\n11: \\n12: create temp table copytest (\\n13: \\tstyle\\ttext,\\n14: \\ttest \\ttext,\\n15: \\tfiller\\tint);\\n16: \\n17: insert into copytest values('DOS',E'abc\\\\r\\\\ndef',1);\\n18: insert into copytest values('Unix',E'abc\\\\ndef',2);\\n19: insert into copytest values('Mac',E'abc\\\\rdef',3);\\n20: insert into copytest values(E'esc\\\\\\\\ape',E'a\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nb',4);\\n21: \\n22: \\\\set filename :abs_builddir '/results/copytest.csv'\\n23: copy copytest to :'filename' csv;\\n24: \\n25: create temp table copytest2 (like copytest);\\n26: \\n27: copy copytest2 from :'filename' csv;\\n28: \\n29: select * from copytest except select * from copytest2;\\n30: \\n31: truncate copytest2;\\n32: \\n33: --- same test but with an escape char different from quote char\\n34: \\n35: copy copytest to :'filename' csv quote '''' escape E'\\\\\\\\';\\n36: \\n37: copy copytest2 from :'filename' csv quote '''' escape E'\\\\\\\\';\\n38: \\n39: select * from copytest except select * from copytest2;\\n40: \\n41: --- test unquoted \\\\. as data inside CSV\\n42: -- do not use copy out to export the data, as it would quote \\\\.\\n43: \\\\o :filename\\n44: \\\\qecho line1\\n45: \\\\qecho '\\\\\\\\.'\\n46: \\\\qecho line2\\n47: \\\\o\\n48: -- get the data back in with copy\\n49: truncate copytest2;\\n50: copy copytest2(test) from :'filename' csv;\\n51: select test from copytest2 order by test collate \\\"C\\\";\\n52: \\n53: -- in text mode, \\\\. must be alone on its line\\n54: truncate copytest2;\\n55: copy copytest2(test) from stdin;\\n56: line1\\n57: line2\\n58: foo\\\\.\\n59: line3\\n60: \\\\.\\n61: copy copytest2(test) from stdin;\\n62: line4\\n63: line5\\n64: \\\\.foo\\n65: line6\\n66: \\\\.\\n67: select test from copytest2;\\n68: \\n69: \\n70: -- test header line feature\\n71: \\n72: create temp table copytest3 (\\n73: \\tc1 int,\\n74: \\t\\\"col with , comma\\\" text,\\n75: \\t\\\"col with \\\"\\\" quote\\\"  int);\\n76: \\n77: copy copytest3 from stdin csv header;\\n78: this is just a line full of junk that would error out if parsed\\n79: 1,a,1\\n80: 2,b,2\\n81: \\\\.\\n82: \\n83: copy copytest3 to stdout csv header;\\n84: \\n85: create temp table copytest4 (\\n86: \\tc1 int,\\n87: \\t\\\"colname with tab: \\t\\\" text);\\n88: \\n89: copy copytest4 from stdin (header);\\n90: this is just a line full of junk that would error out if parsed\\n91: 1\\ta\\n92: 2\\tb\\n93: \\\\.\\n94: \\n95: copy copytest4 to stdout (header);\\n96: \\n97: -- test copy from with a partitioned table\\n98: create table parted_copytest (\\n99: \\ta int,\\n100: \\tb int,\\n101: \\tc text\\n102: ) partition by list (b);\\n103: \\n104: create table parted_copytest_a1 (c text, b int, a int);\\n105: create table parted_copytest_a2 (a int, c text, b int);\\n106: \\n107: alter table parted_copytest attach partition parted_copytest_a1 for values in(1);\\n108: alter table parted_copytest attach partition parted_copytest_a2 for values in(2);\\n109: \\n110: -- We must insert enough rows to trigger multi-inserts.  These are only\\n111: -- enabled adaptively when there are few enough partition changes.\\n112: insert into parted_copytest select x,1,'One' from generate_series(1,1000) x;\\n113: insert into parted_copytest select x,2,'Two' from generate_series(1001,1010) x;\\n114: insert into parted_copytest select x,1,'One' from generate_series(1011,1020) x;\\n115: \\n116: \\\\set filename :abs_builddir '/results/parted_copytest.csv'\\n117: copy (select * from parted_copytest order by a) to :'filename';\\n118: \\n119: truncate parted_copytest;\\n120: \\n121: copy parted_copytest from :'filename';\\n122: \\n123: -- Ensure COPY FREEZE errors for partitioned tables.\\n124: begin;\\n125: truncate parted_copytest;\\n126: copy parted_copytest from :'filename' (freeze);\\n127: rollback;\\n128: \\n129: select tableoid::regclass,count(*),sum(a) from parted_copytest\\n130: group by tableoid order by tableoid::regclass::name;\\n131: \\n132: truncate parted_copytest;\\n133: \\n134: -- create before insert row trigger on parted_copytest_a2\\n135: create function part_ins_func() returns trigger language plpgsql as $$\\n136: begin\\n137:   return new;\\n138: end;\\n139: $$;\\n140: \\n141: create trigger part_ins_trig\\n142: \\tbefore insert on parted_copytest_a2\\n143: \\tfor each row\\n144: \\texecute procedure part_ins_func();\\n145: \\n146: copy parted_copytest from :'filename';\\n147: \\n148: select tableoid::regclass,count(*),sum(a) from parted_copytest\\n149: group by tableoid order by tableoid::regclass::name;\\n150: \\n151: truncate table parted_copytest;\\n152: create index on parted_copytest (b);\\n153: drop trigger part_ins_trig on parted_copytest_a2;\\n154: \\n155: copy parted_copytest from stdin;\\n156: 1\\t1\\tstr1\\n157: 2\\t2\\tstr2\\n158: \\\\.\\n159: \\n160: -- Ensure index entries were properly added during the copy.\\n161: select * from parted_copytest where b = 1;\\n162: select * from parted_copytest where b = 2;\\n163: \\n164: drop table parted_copytest;\\n165: \\n166: --\\n167: -- Progress reporting for COPY\\n168: --\\n169: create table tab_progress_reporting (\\n170: \\tname text,\\n171: \\tage int4,\\n172: \\tlocation point,\\n173: \\tsalary int4,\\n174: \\tmanager name\\n175: );\\n176: \\n177: -- Add a trigger to catch and print the contents of the catalog view\\n178: -- pg_stat_progress_copy during data insertion.  This allows to test\\n179: -- the validation of some progress reports for COPY FROM where the trigger\\n180: -- would fire.\\n181: create function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting() returns trigger AS\\n182: $$\\n183: declare report record;\\n184: begin\\n185:   -- The fields ignored here are the ones that may not remain\\n186:   -- consistent across multiple runs.  The sizes reported may differ\\n187:   -- across platforms, so just check if these are strictly positive.\\n188:   with progress_data as (\\n189:     select\\n190:        relid::regclass::text as relname,\\n191:        command,\\n192:        type,\\n193:        bytes_processed > 0 as has_bytes_processed,\\n194:        bytes_total > 0 as has_bytes_total,\\n195:        tuples_processed,\\n196:        tuples_excluded,\\n197:        tuples_skipped\\n198:       from pg_stat_progress_copy\\n199:       where pid = pg_backend_pid())\\n200:   select into report (to_jsonb(r)) as value\\n201:     from progress_data r;\\n202: \\n203:   raise info 'progress: %', report.value::text;\\n204:   return new;\\n205: end;\\n206: $$ language plpgsql;\\n207: \\n208: create trigger check_after_tab_progress_reporting\\n209: \\tafter insert on tab_progress_reporting\\n210: \\tfor each statement\\n211: \\texecute function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting();\\n212: \\n213: -- Generate COPY FROM report with PIPE.\\n214: copy tab_progress_reporting from stdin;\\n215: sharon\\t25\\t(15,12)\\t1000\\tsam\\n216: sam\\t30\\t(10,5)\\t2000\\tbill\\n217: bill\\t20\\t(11,10)\\t1000\\tsharon\\n218: \\\\.\\n219: \\n220: -- Generate COPY FROM report with FILE, with some excluded tuples.\\n221: truncate tab_progress_reporting;\\n222: \\\\set filename :abs_srcdir '/data/emp.data'\\n223: copy tab_progress_reporting from :'filename'\\n224: \\twhere (salary < 2000);\\n225: \\n226: -- Generate COPY FROM report with PIPE, with some skipped tuples.\\n227: copy tab_progress_reporting from stdin(on_error ignore);\\n228: sharon\\tx\\t(15,12)\\tx\\tsam\\n229: sharon\\t25\\t(15,12)\\t1000\\tsam\\n230: sharon\\ty\\t(15,12)\\tx\\tsam\\n231: \\\\.\\n232: \\n233: drop trigger check_after_tab_progress_reporting on tab_progress_reporting;\\n234: drop function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting();\\n235: drop table tab_progress_reporting;\\n236: \\n237: -- Test header matching feature\\n238: create table header_copytest (\\n239: \\ta int,\\n240: \\tb int,\\n241: \\tc text\\n242: );\\n243: -- Make sure it works with dropped columns\\n244: alter table header_copytest drop column c;\\n245: alter table header_copytest add column c text;\\n246: copy header_copytest to stdout with (header match);\\n247: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header wrong_choice);\\n248: -- works\\n249: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n250: a\\tb\\tc\\n251: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\n252: \\\\.\\n253: copy header_copytest (c, a, b) from stdin with (header match);\\n254: c\\ta\\tb\\n255: bar\\t3\\t4\\n256: \\\\.\\n257: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match, format csv);\\n258: a,b,c\\n259: 5,6,baz\\n260: \\\\.\\n261: -- errors\\n262: copy header_copytest (c, b, a) from stdin with (header match);\\n263: a\\tb\\tc\\n264: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\n265: \\\\.\\n266: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n267: a\\tb\\t\\\\N\\n268: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\n269: \\\\.\\n270: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n271: a\\tb\\n272: 1\\t2\\n273: \\\\.\\n274: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n275: a\\tb\\tc\\td\\n276: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\tbar\\n277: \\\\.\\n278: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n279: a\\tb\\td\\n280: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\n281: \\\\.\\n282: SELECT * FROM header_copytest ORDER BY a;\\n283: \\n284: -- Drop an extra column, in the middle of the existing set.\\n285: alter table header_copytest drop column b;\\n286: -- works\\n287: copy header_copytest (c, a) from stdin with (header match);\\n288: c\\ta\\n289: foo\\t7\\n290: \\\\.\\n291: copy header_copytest (a, c) from stdin with (header match);\\n292: a\\tc\\n293: 8\\tfoo\\n294: \\\\.\\n295: -- errors\\n296: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n297: a\\t........pg.dropped.2........\\tc\\n298: 1\\t2\\tfoo\\n299: \\\\.\\n300: copy header_copytest (a, c) from stdin with (header match);\\n301: a\\tc\\tb\\n302: 1\\tfoo\\t2\\n303: \\\\.\\n304: \\n305: SELECT * FROM header_copytest ORDER BY a;\\n306: drop table header_copytest;\\n307: \\n308: -- test COPY with overlong column defaults\\n309: create temp table oversized_column_default (\\n310:     col1 varchar(5) DEFAULT 'more than 5 chars',\\n311:     col2 varchar(5));\\n312: -- normal COPY should work\\n313: copy oversized_column_default from stdin;\\n314: \\\\.\\n315: -- error if the column is excluded\\n316: copy oversized_column_default (col2) from stdin;\\n317: \\\\.\\n318: -- error if the DEFAULT option is given\\n319: copy oversized_column_default from stdin (default '');\\n320: \\\\.\\n321: drop table oversized_column_default;\\n322: \\n323: \\n324: --\\n325: -- Create partitioned table that does not allow bulk insertions, to test bugs\\n326: -- related to the reuse of BulkInsertState across partitions (only done when\\n327: -- not using bulk insert).  Switching between partitions often makes it more\\n328: -- likely to encounter these bugs, so we just switch on roughly every insert\\n329: -- by having an even/odd number partition and inserting evenly distributed\\n330: -- data.\\n331: --\\n332: CREATE TABLE parted_si (\\n333:   id int not null,\\n334:   data text not null,\\n335:   -- prevent use of bulk insert by having a volatile function\\n336:   rand float8 not null default random()\\n337: )\\n338: PARTITION BY LIST((id % 2));\\n339: \\n340: CREATE TABLE parted_si_p_even PARTITION OF parted_si FOR VALUES IN (0);\\n341: CREATE TABLE parted_si_p_odd PARTITION OF parted_si FOR VALUES IN (1);\\n342: \\n343: -- Test that bulk relation extension handles reusing a single BulkInsertState\\n344: -- across partitions.  Without the fix applied, this reliably reproduces\\n345: -- #18130 unless shared_buffers is extremely small (preventing any use of bulk\\n346: -- relation extension). See\\n347: -- https://postgr.es/m/18130-7a86a7356a75209d%40postgresql.org\\n348: -- https://postgr.es/m/257696.1695670946%40sss.pgh.pa.us\\n349: \\\\set filename :abs_srcdir '/data/desc.data'\\n350: COPY parted_si(id, data) FROM :'filename';\\n351: \\n352: -- An earlier bug (see commit b1ecb9b3fcf) could end up using a buffer from\\n353: -- the wrong partition. This test is *not* guaranteed to trigger that bug, but\\n354: -- does so when shared_buffers is small enough.  To test if we encountered the\\n355: -- bug, check that the partition condition isn't violated.\\n356: SELECT tableoid::regclass, id % 2 = 0 is_even, count(*) from parted_si GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1;\\n357: \\n358: DROP TABLE parted_si;\\n359: \\n360: -- ensure COPY FREEZE errors for foreign tables\\n361: begin;\\n362: create foreign data wrapper copytest_wrapper;\\n363: create server copytest_server foreign data wrapper copytest_wrapper;\\n364: create foreign table copytest_foreign_table (a int) server copytest_server;\\n365: copy copytest_foreign_table from stdin (freeze);\\n366: 1\\n367: \\\\.\\n368: rollback;\\n369: \\n370: -- Tests for COPY TO with materialized views.\\n371: -- COPY TO should fail for an unpopulated materialized view\\n372: -- but succeed for a populated one.\\n373: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv AS SELECT 1 AS id WITH NO DATA;\\n374: COPY copytest_mv(id) TO stdout WITH (header);\\n375: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv;\\n376: COPY copytest_mv(id) TO stdout WITH (header);\\n377: DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv;\\n378: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/test/regress/sql/copy.sql\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: --\n2: -- COPY\n3: --\n4: \n5: -- directory paths are passed to us in environment variables\n6: \\getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR\n7: \\getenv abs_builddir PG_ABS_BUILDDIR\n8: \n9: --- test copying in CSV mode with various styles\n10: --- of embedded line ending characters\n11: \n12: create temp table copytest (\n13: \tstyle\ttext,\n14: \ttest \ttext,\n15: \tfiller\tint);\n16: \n17: insert into copytest values('DOS',E'abc\\r\\ndef',1);\n18: insert into copytest values('Unix',E'abc\\ndef',2);\n19: insert into copytest values('Mac',E'abc\\rdef',3);\n20: insert into copytest values(E'esc\\\\ape',E'a\\\\r\\\\\\r\\\\\\n\\\\nb',4);\n21: \n22: \\set filename :abs_builddir '/results/copytest.csv'\n23: copy copytest to :'filename' csv;\n24: \n25: create temp table copytest2 (like copytest);\n26: \n27: copy copytest2 from :'filename' csv;\n28: \n29: select * from copytest except select * from copytest2;\n30: \n31: truncate copytest2;\n32: \n33: --- same test but with an escape char different from quote char\n34: \n35: copy copytest to :'filename' csv quote '''' escape E'\\\\';\n36: \n37: copy copytest2 from :'filename' csv quote '''' escape E'\\\\';\n38: \n39: select * from copytest except select * from copytest2;\n40: \n41: --- test unquoted \\. as data inside CSV\n42: -- do not use copy out to export the data, as it would quote \\.\n43: \\o :filename\n44: \\qecho line1\n45: \\qecho '\\\\.'\n46: \\qecho line2\n47: \\o\n48: -- get the data back in with copy\n49: truncate copytest2;\n50: copy copytest2(test) from :'filename' csv;\n51: select test from copytest2 order by test collate \"C\";\n52: \n53: -- in text mode, \\. must be alone on its line\n54: truncate copytest2;\n55: copy copytest2(test) from stdin;\n56: line1\n57: line2\n58: foo\\.\n59: line3\n60: \\.\n61: copy copytest2(test) from stdin;\n62: line4\n63: line5\n64: \\.foo\n65: line6\n66: \\.\n67: select test from copytest2;\n68: \n69: \n70: -- test header line feature\n71: \n72: create temp table copytest3 (\n73: \tc1 int,\n74: \t\"col with , comma\" text,\n75: \t\"col with \"\" quote\"  int);\n76: \n77: copy copytest3 from stdin csv header;\n78: this is just a line full of junk that would error out if parsed\n79: 1,a,1\n80: 2,b,2\n81: \\.\n82: \n83: copy copytest3 to stdout csv header;\n84: \n85: create temp table copytest4 (\n86: \tc1 int,\n87: \t\"colname with tab: \t\" text);\n88: \n89: copy copytest4 from stdin (header);\n90: this is just a line full of junk that would error out if parsed\n91: 1\ta\n92: 2\tb\n93: \\.\n94: \n95: copy copytest4 to stdout (header);\n96: \n97: -- test copy from with a partitioned table\n98: create table parted_copytest (\n99: \ta int,\n100: \tb int,\n101: \tc text\n102: ) partition by list (b);\n103: \n104: create table parted_copytest_a1 (c text, b int, a int);\n105: create table parted_copytest_a2 (a int, c text, b int);\n106: \n107: alter table parted_copytest attach partition parted_copytest_a1 for values in(1);\n108: alter table parted_copytest attach partition parted_copytest_a2 for values in(2);\n109: \n110: -- We must insert enough rows to trigger multi-inserts.  These are only\n111: -- enabled adaptively when there are few enough partition changes.\n112: insert into parted_copytest select x,1,'One' from generate_series(1,1000) x;\n113: insert into parted_copytest select x,2,'Two' from generate_series(1001,1010) x;\n114: insert into parted_copytest select x,1,'One' from generate_series(1011,1020) x;\n115: \n116: \\set filename :abs_builddir '/results/parted_copytest.csv'\n117: copy (select * from parted_copytest order by a) to :'filename';\n118: \n119: truncate parted_copytest;\n120: \n121: copy parted_copytest from :'filename';\n122: \n123: -- Ensure COPY FREEZE errors for partitioned tables.\n124: begin;\n125: truncate parted_copytest;\n126: copy parted_copytest from :'filename' (freeze);\n127: rollback;\n128: \n129: select tableoid::regclass,count(*),sum(a) from parted_copytest\n130: group by tableoid order by tableoid::regclass::name;\n131: \n132: truncate parted_copytest;\n133: \n134: -- create before insert row trigger on parted_copytest_a2\n135: create function part_ins_func() returns trigger language plpgsql as $$\n136: begin\n137:   return new;\n138: end;\n139: $$;\n140: \n141: create trigger part_ins_trig\n142: \tbefore insert on parted_copytest_a2\n143: \tfor each row\n144: \texecute procedure part_ins_func();\n145: \n146: copy parted_copytest from :'filename';\n147: \n148: select tableoid::regclass,count(*),sum(a) from parted_copytest\n149: group by tableoid order by tableoid::regclass::name;\n150: \n151: truncate table parted_copytest;\n152: create index on parted_copytest (b);\n153: drop trigger part_ins_trig on parted_copytest_a2;\n154: \n155: copy parted_copytest from stdin;\n156: 1\t1\tstr1\n157: 2\t2\tstr2\n158: \\.\n159: \n160: -- Ensure index entries were properly added during the copy.\n161: select * from parted_copytest where b = 1;\n162: select * from parted_copytest where b = 2;\n163: \n164: drop table parted_copytest;\n165: \n166: --\n167: -- Progress reporting for COPY\n168: --\n169: create table tab_progress_reporting (\n170: \tname text,\n171: \tage int4,\n172: \tlocation point,\n173: \tsalary int4,\n174: \tmanager name\n175: );\n176: \n177: -- Add a trigger to catch and print the contents of the catalog view\n178: -- pg_stat_progress_copy during data insertion.  This allows to test\n179: -- the validation of some progress reports for COPY FROM where the trigger\n180: -- would fire.\n181: create function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting() returns trigger AS\n182: $$\n183: declare report record;\n184: begin\n185:   -- The fields ignored here are the ones that may not remain\n186:   -- consistent across multiple runs.  The sizes reported may differ\n187:   -- across platforms, so just check if these are strictly positive.\n188:   with progress_data as (\n189:     select\n190:        relid::regclass::text as relname,\n191:        command,\n192:        type,\n193:        bytes_processed > 0 as has_bytes_processed,\n194:        bytes_total > 0 as has_bytes_total,\n195:        tuples_processed,\n196:        tuples_excluded,\n197:        tuples_skipped\n198:       from pg_stat_progress_copy\n199:       where pid = pg_backend_pid())\n200:   select into report (to_jsonb(r)) as value\n201:     from progress_data r;\n202: \n203:   raise info 'progress: %', report.value::text;\n204:   return new;\n205: end;\n206: $$ language plpgsql;\n207: \n208: create trigger check_after_tab_progress_reporting\n209: \tafter insert on tab_progress_reporting\n210: \tfor each statement\n211: \texecute function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting();\n212: \n213: -- Generate COPY FROM report with PIPE.\n214: copy tab_progress_reporting from stdin;\n215: sharon\t25\t(15,12)\t1000\tsam\n216: sam\t30\t(10,5)\t2000\tbill\n217: bill\t20\t(11,10)\t1000\tsharon\n218: \\.\n219: \n220: -- Generate COPY FROM report with FILE, with some excluded tuples.\n221: truncate tab_progress_reporting;\n222: \\set filename :abs_srcdir '/data/emp.data'\n223: copy tab_progress_reporting from :'filename'\n224: \twhere (salary < 2000);\n225: \n226: -- Generate COPY FROM report with PIPE, with some skipped tuples.\n227: copy tab_progress_reporting from stdin(on_error ignore);\n228: sharon\tx\t(15,12)\tx\tsam\n229: sharon\t25\t(15,12)\t1000\tsam\n230: sharon\ty\t(15,12)\tx\tsam\n231: \\.\n232: \n233: drop trigger check_after_tab_progress_reporting on tab_progress_reporting;\n234: drop function notice_after_tab_progress_reporting();\n235: drop table tab_progress_reporting;\n236: \n237: -- Test header matching feature\n238: create table header_copytest (\n239: \ta int,\n240: \tb int,\n241: \tc text\n242: );\n243: -- Make sure it works with dropped columns\n244: alter table header_copytest drop column c;\n245: alter table header_copytest add column c text;\n246: copy header_copytest to stdout with (header match);\n247: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header wrong_choice);\n248: -- works\n249: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n250: a\tb\tc\n251: 1\t2\tfoo\n252: \\.\n253: copy header_copytest (c, a, b) from stdin with (header match);\n254: c\ta\tb\n255: bar\t3\t4\n256: \\.\n257: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match, format csv);\n258: a,b,c\n259: 5,6,baz\n260: \\.\n261: -- errors\n262: copy header_copytest (c, b, a) from stdin with (header match);\n263: a\tb\tc\n264: 1\t2\tfoo\n265: \\.\n266: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n267: a\tb\t\\N\n268: 1\t2\tfoo\n269: \\.\n270: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n271: a\tb\n272: 1\t2\n273: \\.\n274: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n275: a\tb\tc\td\n276: 1\t2\tfoo\tbar\n277: \\.\n278: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n279: a\tb\td\n280: 1\t2\tfoo\n281: \\.\n282: SELECT * FROM header_copytest ORDER BY a;\n283: \n284: -- Drop an extra column, in the middle of the existing set.\n285: alter table header_copytest drop column b;\n286: -- works\n287: copy header_copytest (c, a) from stdin with (header match);\n288: c\ta\n289: foo\t7\n290: \\.\n291: copy header_copytest (a, c) from stdin with (header match);\n292: a\tc\n293: 8\tfoo\n294: \\.\n295: -- errors\n296: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n297: a\t........pg.dropped.2........\tc\n298: 1\t2\tfoo\n299: \\.\n300: copy header_copytest (a, c) from stdin with (header match);\n301: a\tc\tb\n302: 1\tfoo\t2\n303: \\.\n304: \n305: SELECT * FROM header_copytest ORDER BY a;\n306: drop table header_copytest;\n307: \n308: -- test COPY with overlong column defaults\n309: create temp table oversized_column_default (\n310:     col1 varchar(5) DEFAULT 'more than 5 chars',\n311:     col2 varchar(5));\n312: -- normal COPY should work\n313: copy oversized_column_default from stdin;\n314: \\.\n315: -- error if the column is excluded\n316: copy oversized_column_default (col2) from stdin;\n317: \\.\n318: -- error if the DEFAULT option is given\n319: copy oversized_column_default from stdin (default '');\n320: \\.\n321: drop table oversized_column_default;\n322: \n323: \n324: --\n325: -- Create partitioned table that does not allow bulk insertions, to test bugs\n326: -- related to the reuse of BulkInsertState across partitions (only done when\n327: -- not using bulk insert).  Switching between partitions often makes it more\n328: -- likely to encounter these bugs, so we just switch on roughly every insert\n329: -- by having an even/odd number partition and inserting evenly distributed\n330: -- data.\n331: --\n332: CREATE TABLE parted_si (\n333:   id int not null,\n334:   data text not null,\n335:   -- prevent use of bulk insert by having a volatile function\n336:   rand float8 not null default random()\n337: )\n338: PARTITION BY LIST((id % 2));\n339: \n340: CREATE TABLE parted_si_p_even PARTITION OF parted_si FOR VALUES IN (0);\n341: CREATE TABLE parted_si_p_odd PARTITION OF parted_si FOR VALUES IN (1);\n342: \n343: -- Test that bulk relation extension handles reusing a single BulkInsertState\n344: -- across partitions.  Without the fix applied, this reliably reproduces\n345: -- #18130 unless shared_buffers is extremely small (preventing any use of bulk\n346: -- relation extension). See\n347: -- https://postgr.es/m/18130-7a86a7356a75209d%40postgresql.org\n348: -- https://postgr.es/m/257696.1695670946%40sss.pgh.pa.us\n349: \\set filename :abs_srcdir '/data/desc.data'\n350: COPY parted_si(id, data) FROM :'filename';\n351: \n352: -- An earlier bug (see commit b1ecb9b3fcf) could end up using a buffer from\n353: -- the wrong partition. This test is *not* guaranteed to trigger that bug, but\n354: -- does so when shared_buffers is small enough.  To test if we encountered the\n355: -- bug, check that the partition condition isn't violated.\n356: SELECT tableoid::regclass, id % 2 = 0 is_even, count(*) from parted_si GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1;\n357: \n358: DROP TABLE parted_si;\n359: \n360: -- ensure COPY FREEZE errors for foreign tables\n361: begin;\n362: create foreign data wrapper copytest_wrapper;\n363: create server copytest_server foreign data wrapper copytest_wrapper;\n364: create foreign table copytest_foreign_table (a int) server copytest_server;\n365: copy copytest_foreign_table from stdin (freeze);\n366: 1\n367: \\.\n368: rollback;\n369: \n370: -- Tests for COPY TO with materialized views.\n371: -- COPY TO should fail for an unpopulated materialized view\n372: -- but succeed for a populated one.\n373: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv AS SELECT 1 AS id WITH NO DATA;\n374: COPY copytest_mv(id) TO stdout WITH (header);\n375: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv;\n376: COPY copytest_mv(id) TO stdout WITH (header);\n377: DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW copytest_mv;\n378: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/test/regress/sql/copy.sql"
  }
}

49. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

Input
{
  "endLine": 50,
  "path": "src/backend/parser/gram.y",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: %{\\n2: \\n3: /*#define YYDEBUG 1*/\\n4: /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n5:  *\\n6:  * gram.y\\n7:  *\\t  POSTGRESQL BISON rules/actions\\n8:  *\\n9:  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group\\n10:  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California\\n11:  *\\n12:  *\\n13:  * IDENTIFICATION\\n14:  *\\t  src/backend/parser/gram.y\\n15:  *\\n16:  * HISTORY\\n17:  *\\t  AUTHOR\\t\\t\\tDATE\\t\\t\\tMAJOR EVENT\\n18:  *\\t  Andrew Yu\\t\\t\\tSept, 1994\\t\\tPOSTQUEL to SQL conversion\\n19:  *\\t  Andrew Yu\\t\\t\\tOct, 1994\\t\\tlispy code conversion\\n20:  *\\n21:  * NOTES\\n22:  *\\t  CAPITALS are used to represent terminal symbols.\\n23:  *\\t  non-capitals are used to represent non-terminals.\\n24:  *\\n25:  *\\t  In general, nothing in this file should initiate database accesses\\n26:  *\\t  nor depend on changeable state (such as SET variables).  If you do\\n27:  *\\t  database accesses, your code will fail when we have aborted the\\n28:  *\\t  current transaction and are just parsing commands to find the next\\n29:  *\\t  ROLLBACK or COMMIT.  If you make use of SET variables, then you\\n30:  *\\t  will do the wrong thing in multi-query strings like this:\\n31:  *\\t\\t\\tSET constraint_exclusion TO off; SELECT * FROM foo;\\n32:  *\\t  because the entire string is parsed by gram.y before the SET gets\\n33:  *\\t  executed.  Anything that depends on the database or changeable state\\n34:  *\\t  should be handled during parse analysis so that it happens at the\\n35:  *\\t  right time not the wrong time.\\n36:  *\\n37:  * WARNINGS\\n38:  *\\t  If you use a list, make sure the datum is a node so that the printing\\n39:  *\\t  routines work.\\n40:  *\\n41:  *\\t  Sometimes we assign constants to makeStrings. Make sure we don't free\\n42:  *\\t  those.\\n43:  *\\n44:  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n45:  */\\n46: #include \\\"postgres.h\\\"\\n47: \\n48: #include <ctype.h>\\n49: #include <limits.h>\\n50: \\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/parser/gram.y%3FL1-50\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: %{\n2: \n3: /*#define YYDEBUG 1*/\n4: /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n5:  *\n6:  * gram.y\n7:  *\t  POSTGRESQL BISON rules/actions\n8:  *\n9:  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group\n10:  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California\n11:  *\n12:  *\n13:  * IDENTIFICATION\n14:  *\t  src/backend/parser/gram.y\n15:  *\n16:  * HISTORY\n17:  *\t  AUTHOR\t\t\tDATE\t\t\tMAJOR EVENT\n18:  *\t  Andrew Yu\t\t\tSept, 1994\t\tPOSTQUEL to SQL conversion\n19:  *\t  Andrew Yu\t\t\tOct, 1994\t\tlispy code conversion\n20:  *\n21:  * NOTES\n22:  *\t  CAPITALS are used to represent terminal symbols.\n23:  *\t  non-capitals are used to represent non-terminals.\n24:  *\n25:  *\t  In general, nothing in this file should initiate database accesses\n26:  *\t  nor depend on changeable state (such as SET variables).  If you do\n27:  *\t  database accesses, your code will fail when we have aborted the\n28:  *\t  current transaction and are just parsing commands to find the next\n29:  *\t  ROLLBACK or COMMIT.  If you make use of SET variables, then you\n30:  *\t  will do the wrong thing in multi-query strings like this:\n31:  *\t\t\tSET constraint_exclusion TO off; SELECT * FROM foo;\n32:  *\t  because the entire string is parsed by gram.y before the SET gets\n33:  *\t  executed.  Anything that depends on the database or changeable state\n34:  *\t  should be handled during parse analysis so that it happens at the\n35:  *\t  right time not the wrong time.\n36:  *\n37:  * WARNINGS\n38:  *\t  If you use a list, make sure the datum is a node so that the printing\n39:  *\t  routines work.\n40:  *\n41:  *\t  Sometimes we assign constants to makeStrings. Make sure we don't free\n42:  *\t  those.\n43:  *\n44:  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n45:  */\n46: #include \"postgres.h\"\n47: \n48: #include <ctype.h>\n49: #include <limits.h>\n50: \n51: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/backend/parser/gram.y%3FL1-50"
  }
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "1828:opt_boolean_or_string:\n1829-\t\t\tTRUE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"true\"; }\n1830-\t\t\t| FALSE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"false\"; }\n1831-\t\t\t| ON\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"on\"; }\n1832-\t\t\t/*\n1833-\t\t\t * OFF is also accepted as a boolean value, but is handled by\n1834-\t\t\t * the NonReservedWord rule.  The action for booleans and strings\n1835-\t\t\t * is the same, so we don't need to distinguish them here.\n1836-\t\t\t */\n1837-\t\t\t| NonReservedWord_or_Sconst\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n1838-\t\t;\n1839-\n1840-/* Timezone values can be:\n1841- * - a string such as 'pst8pdt'\n1842- * - an identifier such as \"pst8pdt\"\n1843- * - an integer or floating point number\n1844- * - a time interval per SQL99\n1845- * ColId gives reduce/reduce errors against ConstInterval and LOCAL,\n1846- * so use IDENT (meaning we reject anything that is a key word).\n1847- */\n1848-zone_value:"
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"215: -- Test header matching feature\\n216: create table header_copytest (\\n217: \\ta int,\\n218: \\tb int,\\n219: \\tc text\\n220: );\\n221: -- Make sure it works with dropped columns\\n222: alter table header_copytest drop column c;\\n223: alter table header_copytest add column c text;\\n224: copy header_copytest to stdout with (header match);\\n225: ERROR:  cannot use \\\"match\\\" with HEADER in COPY TO\\n226: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header wrong_choice);\\n227: ERROR:  header requires a Boolean value or \\\"match\\\"\\n228: -- works\\n229: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\\n230: copy header_copytest (c, a, b) from stdin with (header match);\\n231: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match, format csv);\\n232: -- errors\\n233: copy header_copytest (c, b, a) from stdin with (header match);\\n234: ERROR:  column name mismatch in header line field 1: got \\\"a\\\", expected \\\"c\\\"\\n235: CONTEXT:  COPY header_copytest, line 1: \\\"a\\tb\\tc\\\"\\n236: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/test/regress/expected/copy.out%3FL215-235\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "215: -- Test header matching feature\n216: create table header_copytest (\n217: \ta int,\n218: \tb int,\n219: \tc text\n220: );\n221: -- Make sure it works with dropped columns\n222: alter table header_copytest drop column c;\n223: alter table header_copytest add column c text;\n224: copy header_copytest to stdout with (header match);\n225: ERROR:  cannot use \"match\" with HEADER in COPY TO\n226: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header wrong_choice);\n227: ERROR:  header requires a Boolean value or \"match\"\n228: -- works\n229: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match);\n230: copy header_copytest (c, a, b) from stdin with (header match);\n231: copy header_copytest from stdin with (header match, format csv);\n232: -- errors\n233: copy header_copytest (c, b, a) from stdin with (header match);\n234: ERROR:  column name mismatch in header line field 1: got \"a\", expected \"c\"\n235: CONTEXT:  COPY header_copytest, line 1: \"a\tb\tc\"\n236: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4@eb8f07549ee15f0db57589c5f333de3f874fe855/-/blob/src/test/regress/expected/copy.out%3FL215-235"
  }

65. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore the PostgreSQL source and test files",
  "prompt": "Use sg_list_files to explore the following directories in the github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4 repository and provide a summary of what files are available:\n1. src/backend/parser/ - for gram.y and related\n2. src/backend/commands/ - for copy*.c files\n3. src/include/commands/ - for copy.h\n4. src/include/parser/ - for kwlist.h\n5. src/test/regress/sql/ and src/test/regress/expected/ - for test files\n\nReport the key files that need to be modified for the COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH feature.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a536ffffc451c2f79",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! I've successfully explored all the requested directories. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of the key files that need to be modified for the COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH feature:\n\n## Summary of Available Files for COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH Feature\n\n### 1. Parser Directory (`src/backend/parser/`)\n- **gram.y** - The main grammar file where COPY syntax is defined\n- **scan.l** - Lexical scanner for tokenization\n- **parse_utilcmd.c** - Utility command parsing (where COPY is parsed)\n- Supporting parser files: analyze.c, parse_clause.c, parse_expr.c, etc.\n\n### 2. Commands Directory (`src/backend/commands/`)\nKey COPY-related files:\n- **copy.c** - Main COPY command entry point\n- **copyfrom.c** - COPY FROM implementation\n- **copyfromparse.c** - COPY FROM parsing logic\n- **copyto.c** - COPY TO implementation\n\n### 3. Header Files (`src/include/commands/`)\n- **copy.h** - COPY command structures and declarations\n- **copyfrom_internal.h** - Internal COPY FROM structures\n- **copyapi.h** - Public COPY API\n\n### 4. Parser Headers (`src/include/parser/`)\n- **kwlist.h** - Keyword list (where new keywords like HEADER_MATCH would be added)\n- **parsetree.h** - Parse tree node structures\n- **parser.h** - Parser declarations\n\n### 5. Test Files\n**SQL Test Files** (`src/test/regress/sql/`):\n- **copy.sql** - Main COPY tests\n- **copy2.sql** - Additional COPY tests\n- **copydml.sql** - COPY with DML tests\n- **copyencoding.sql** - COPY encoding tests\n- **copyselect.sql** - COPY SELECT tests\n\n**Expected Output Files** (`src/test/regress/expected/`):\n- **copy.out** - Expected output for copy.sql\n- **copy2.out** - Expected output for copy2.sql\n- **copydml.out** - Expected output for copydml.sql\n- **copyencoding.out** - Expected output for copyencoding.sql\n- **copyselect.out** - Expected output for copyselect.sql\n\n## Key Files to Modify for COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH\n\nFor implementing this feature, you would primarily need to modify:\n\n1. **/src/backend/parser/gram.y** - Add syntax for HEADER_MATCH option\n2. **/src/backend/commands/copyfromparse.c** - Parse the HEADER_MATCH clause and validate CSV header\n3. **/src/backend/commands/copyfrom.c** - Implement the header matching logic during data import\n4. **/src/include/commands/copy.h** - Add data structures to store header match parameters\n5. **/src/include/commands/copyfrom_internal.h** - Add internal structures for header processing\n6. **/src/include/parser/kwlist.h** - Add new keywords if needed\n7. **/src/test/regress/sql/copy.sql** (or create new test file) - Add test cases\n8. **/src/test/regress/expected/copy.out** - Add expected test results\n\nThese files form the complete implementation pathway for the COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH feature, covering parsing, execution, internal data structures, and testing.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Use sg_list_files to explore the following directories in the github.com/sg-evals/postgres--5a461dc4 repository and provide a summary of what files are available:\n1. src/backend/parser/ - for gram.y and related\n2. src/backend/commands/ - for copy*.c files\n3. src/include/commands/ - for copy.h\n4. src/include/parser/ - for kwlist.h\n5. src/test/regress/sql/ and src/test/regress/expected/ - for test files\n\nReport the key files that need to be modified for the COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH feature.",

72. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/src/backend/commands"
}
Output
"Error: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read '/workspace/src/backend/commands'"
73. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/summary.md << 'EOF'\n# COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH Implementation Status\n\n## What's Already Implemented:\n\n### 1. Data Structures (copy.h)\n- \u2705 `CopyHeaderChoice` enum with values:\n  - COPY_HEADER_FALSE = 0\n  - COPY_HEADER_TRUE = 1\n  - COPY_HEADER_MATCH = 2\n\n### 2. Option Processing (copy.c)\n- \u2705 `defGetCopyHeaderChoice()` function\n  - Parses \"match\" string to COPY_HEADER_MATCH\n  - Validates HEADER MATCH only for COPY FROM (not COPY TO)\n  - Accepts 0, 1, \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", \"match\"\n\n### 3. Header Validation (copyfromparse.c)\n- \u2705 `NextCopyFromRawFieldsInternal()` function\n  - Checks if header_line == COPY_HEADER_MATCH (line 790)\n  - Validates field count matches expected columns\n  - Case-sensitive comparison of column names using namestrcmp\n\n### 4. Tests (copy.sql)\n- \u2705 Tests already present (lines 237-305)\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with matching headers\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with mismatched headers\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with wrong column count\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with column reordering\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with dropped columns\n\n### 5. Expected Output (copy.out)\n- \u2705 Expected results already present\n\n## What Needs Verification:\n\n### 1. Grammar Support\nThe new COPY syntax `with (header match)` should work through:\n- `copy_generic_opt_list` \u2192 `copy_generic_opt_elem` \u2192 ColLabel copy_generic_opt_arg\n- ColLabel = \"header\", copy_generic_opt_arg = \"match\"\n- opt_boolean_or_string accepts NonReservedWord_or_Sconst\n- MATCH is UNRESERVED_KEYWORD\n\nBut the old syntax `COPY ... HEADER MATCH` might not be supported in copy_opt_item.\n\n### 2. Edge Cases to Verify\n- HEADER MATCH with COPY TO should error\n- HEADER MATCH with BINARY format should error\n- HEADER MATCH case-insensitive comparison\n\n## Next Steps:\n1. Add HEADER_P MATCH rule to copy_opt_item for completeness (if needed)\n2. Verify all tests pass\n3. Check error handling for edge cases\nEOF\ncat /tmp/summary.md\n",
  "description": "Create summary of implementation status"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "# COPY FROM CSV WITH HEADER MATCH Implementation Status\n\n## What's Already Implemented:\n\n### 1. Data Structures (copy.h)\n- \u2705 `CopyHeaderChoice` enum with values:\n  - COPY_HEADER_FALSE = 0\n  - COPY_HEADER_TRUE = 1\n  - COPY_HEADER_MATCH = 2\n\n### 2. Option Processing (copy.c)\n- \u2705 `defGetCopyHeaderChoice()` function\n  - Parses \"match\" string to COPY_HEADER_MATCH\n  - Validates HEADER MATCH only for COPY FROM (not COPY TO)\n  - Accepts 0, 1, \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", \"match\"\n\n### 3. Header Validation (copyfromparse.c)\n- \u2705 `NextCopyFromRawFieldsInternal()` function\n  - Checks if header_line == COPY_HEADER_MATCH (line 790)\n  - Validates field count matches expected columns\n  - Case-sensitive comparison of column names using namestrcmp\n\n### 4. Tests (copy.sql)\n- \u2705 Tests already present (lines 237-305)\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with matching headers\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with mismatched headers\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with wrong column count\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with column reordering\n  - Test HEADER MATCH with dropped columns\n\n### 5. Expected Output (copy.out)\n- \u2705 Expected results already present\n\n## What Needs Verification:\n\n### 1. Grammar Support\nThe new COPY syntax `with (header match)` should work through:\n- `copy_generic_opt_list` \u2192 `copy_generic_opt_elem` \u2192 ColLabel copy_generic_opt_arg\n- ColLabel = \"header\", copy_generic_opt_arg = \"match\"\n- opt_boolean_or_string accepts NonReservedWord_or_Sconst\n- MATCH is UNRESERVED_KEYWORD\n\nBut the old syntax `COPY ... HEADER MATCH` might not be supported in copy_opt_item.\n\n### 2. Edge Cases to Verify\n- HEADER MATCH with COPY TO should error\n- HEADER MATCH with BINARY format should error\n- HEADER MATCH case-insensitive comparison\n\n## Next Steps:\n1. Add HEADER_P MATCH rule to copy_opt_item for completeness (if needed)\n2. Verify all tests pass\n3. Check error handling for edge cases"
}

  "oldString": "\t\t\t| HEADER_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"header\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| QUOTE opt_as Sconst",
  "originalFile": "%{\n\n/*#define YYDEBUG 1*/\n/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n *\n * gram.y\n *\t  POSTGRESQL BISON rules/actions\n *\n * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group\n * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California\n *\n *\n * IDENTIFICATION\n *\t  src/backend/parser/gram.y\n *\n * HISTORY\n *\t  AUTHOR\t\t\tDATE\t\t\tMAJOR EVENT\n *\t  Andrew Yu\t\t\tSept, 1994\t\tPOSTQUEL to SQL conversion\n *\t  Andrew Yu\t\t\tOct, 1994\t\tlispy code conversion\n *\n * NOTES\n *\t  CAPITALS are used to represent terminal symbols.\n *\t  non-capitals are used to represent non-terminals.\n *\n *\t  In general, nothing in this file should initiate database accesses\n *\t  nor depend on changeable state (such as SET variables).  If you do\n *\t  database accesses, your code will fail when we have aborted the\n *\t  current transaction and are just parsing commands to find the next\n *\t  ROLLBACK or COMMIT.  If you make use of SET variables, then you\n *\t  will do the wrong thing in multi-query strings like this:\n *\t\t\tSET constraint_exclusion TO off; SELECT * FROM foo;\n *\t  because the entire string is parsed by gram.y before the SET gets\n *\t  executed.  Anything that depends on the database or changeable state\n *\t  should be handled during parse analysis so that it happens at the\n *\t  right time not the wrong time.\n *\n * WARNINGS\n *\t  If you use a list, make sure the datum is a node so that the printing\n *\t  routines work.\n *\n *\t  Sometimes we assign constants to makeStrings. Make sure we don't free\n *\t  those.\n *\n *-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\n#include \"postgres.h\"\n\n#include <ctype.h>\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#include \"catalog/index.h\"\n#include \"catalog/namespace.h\"\n#include \"catalog/pg_am.h\"\n#include \"catalog/pg_trigger.h\"\n#include \"commands/defrem.h\"\n#include \"commands/trigger.h\"\n#include \"gramparse.h\"\n#include \"nodes/makefuncs.h\"\n#include \"nodes/nodeFuncs.h\"\n#include \"parser/parser.h\"\n#include \"utils/datetime.h\"\n#include \"utils/xml.h\"\n\n\n/*\n * Location tracking support.  Unlike bison's default, we only want\n * to track the start position not the end position of each nonterminal.\n * Nonterminals that reduce to empty receive position \"-1\".  Since a\n * production's leading RHS nonterminal(s) may have reduced to empty,\n * we have to scan to find the first one that's not -1.\n */\n#define YYLLOC_DEFAULT(Current, Rhs, N) \\\n\tdo { \\\n\t\t(Current) = (-1); \\\n\t\tfor (int _i = 1; _i <= (N); _i++) \\\n\t\t{ \\\n\t\t\tif ((Rhs)[_i] >= 0) \\\n\t\t\t{ \\\n\t\t\t\t(Current) = (Rhs)[_i]; \\\n\t\t\t\tbreak; \\\n\t\t\t} \\\n\t\t} \\\n\t} while (0)\n\n/*\n * Bison doesn't allocate anything that needs to live across parser calls,\n * so we can easily have it use palloc instead of malloc.  This prevents\n * memory leaks if we error out during parsing.\n */\n#define YYMALLOC palloc\n#define YYFREE   pfree\n\n/* Private struct for the result of privilege_target production */\ntypedef struct PrivTarget\n{\n\tGrantTargetType targtype;\n\tObjectType\tobjtype;\n\tList\t   *objs;\n} PrivTarget;\n\n/* Private struct for the result of import_qualification production */\ntypedef struct ImportQual\n{\n\tImportForeignSchemaType type;\n\tList\t   *table_names;\n} ImportQual;\n\n/* Private struct for the result of select_limit & limit_clause productions */\ntypedef struct SelectLimit\n{\n\tNode\t   *limitOffset;\n\tNode\t   *limitCount;\n\tLimitOption limitOption;\t/* indicates presence of WITH TIES */\n\tParseLoc\toffsetLoc;\t\t/* location of OFFSET token, if present */\n\tParseLoc\tcountLoc;\t\t/* location of LIMIT/FETCH token, if present */\n\tParseLoc\toptionLoc;\t\t/* location of WITH TIES, if present */\n} SelectLimit;\n\n/* Private struct for the result of group_clause production */\ntypedef struct GroupClause\n{\n\tbool\t\tdistinct;\n\tList\t   *list;\n} GroupClause;\n\n/* Private structs for the result of key_actions and key_action productions */\ntypedef struct KeyAction\n{\n\tchar\t\taction;\n\tList\t   *cols;\n} KeyAction;\n\ntypedef struct KeyActions\n{\n\tKeyAction *updateAction;\n\tKeyAction *deleteAction;\n} KeyActions;\n\n/* ConstraintAttributeSpec yields an integer bitmask of these flags: */\n#define CAS_NOT_DEFERRABLE\t\t\t0x01\n#define CAS_DEFERRABLE\t\t\t\t0x02\n#define CAS_INITIALLY_IMMEDIATE\t\t0x04\n#define CAS_INITIALLY_DEFERRED\t\t0x08\n#define CAS_NOT_VALID\t\t\t\t0x10\n#define CAS_NO_INHERIT\t\t\t\t0x20\n#define CAS_NOT_ENFORCED\t\t\t0x40\n#define CAS_ENFORCED\t\t\t\t0x80\n\n\n#define parser_yyerror(msg)  scanner_yyerror(msg, yyscanner)\n#define parser_errposition(pos)  scanner_errposition(pos, yyscanner)\n\nstatic void base_yyerror(YYLTYPE *yylloc, core_yyscan_t yyscanner,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t const char *msg);\nstatic RawStmt *makeRawStmt(Node *stmt, int stmt_location);\nstatic void updateRawStmtEnd(RawStmt *rs, int end_location);\nstatic Node *makeColumnRef(char *colname, List *indirection,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t   int location, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic Node *makeTypeCast(Node *arg, TypeName *typename, int location);\nstatic Node *makeStringConstCast(char *str, int location, TypeName *typename);\nstatic Node *makeIntConst(int val, int location);\nstatic Node *makeFloatConst(char *str, int location);\nstatic Node *makeBoolAConst(bool state, int location);\nstatic Node *makeBitStringConst(char *str, int location);\nstatic Node *makeNullAConst(int location);\nstatic Node *makeAConst(Node *v, int location);\nstatic RoleSpec *makeRoleSpec(RoleSpecType type, int location);\nstatic void check_qualified_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic List *check_func_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic List *check_indirection(List *indirection, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic List *extractArgTypes(List *parameters);\nstatic List *extractAggrArgTypes(List *aggrargs);\nstatic List *makeOrderedSetArgs(List *directargs, List *orderedargs,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcore_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic void insertSelectOptions(SelectStmt *stmt,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tList *sortClause, List *lockingClause,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *limitClause,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tWithClause *withClause,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcore_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic Node *makeSetOp(SetOperation op, bool all, Node *larg, Node *rarg);\nstatic Node *doNegate(Node *n, int location);\nstatic void doNegateFloat(Float *v);\nstatic Node *makeAndExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location);\nstatic Node *makeOrExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location);\nstatic Node *makeNotExpr(Node *expr, int location);\nstatic Node *makeAArrayExpr(List *elements, int location, int end_location);\nstatic Node *makeSQLValueFunction(SQLValueFunctionOp op, int32 typmod,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  int location);\nstatic Node *makeXmlExpr(XmlExprOp op, char *name, List *named_args,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t List *args, int location);\nstatic List *mergeTableFuncParameters(List *func_args, List *columns, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic TypeName *TableFuncTypeName(List *columns);\nstatic RangeVar *makeRangeVarFromAnyName(List *names, int position, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic RangeVar *makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName(char *name, List *namelist, int location,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic void SplitColQualList(List *qualList,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t List **constraintList, CollateClause **collClause,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic void processCASbits(int cas_bits, int location, const char *constrType,\n\t\t\t   bool *deferrable, bool *initdeferred, bool *is_enforced,\n\t\t\t   bool *not_valid, bool *no_inherit, core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic PartitionStrategy parsePartitionStrategy(char *strategy, int location,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcore_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic void preprocess_pubobj_list(List *pubobjspec_list,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   core_yyscan_t yyscanner);\nstatic Node *makeRecursiveViewSelect(char *relname, List *aliases, Node *query);\n\n%}\n\n%pure-parser\n%expect 0\n%name-prefix=\"base_yy\"\n%locations\n\n%parse-param {core_yyscan_t yyscanner}\n%lex-param   {core_yyscan_t yyscanner}\n\n%union\n{\n\tcore_YYSTYPE core_yystype;\n\t/* these fields must match core_YYSTYPE: */\n\tint\t\t\tival;\n\tchar\t   *str;\n\tconst char *keyword;\n\n\tchar\t\tchr;\n\tbool\t\tboolean;\n\tJoinType\tjtype;\n\tDropBehavior dbehavior;\n\tOnCommitAction oncommit;\n\tList\t   *list;\n\tNode\t   *node;\n\tObjectType\tobjtype;\n\tTypeName   *typnam;\n\tFunctionParameter *fun_param;\n\tFunctionParameterMode fun_param_mode;\n\tObjectWithArgs *objwithargs;\n\tDefElem\t   *defelt;\n\tSortBy\t   *sortby;\n\tWindowDef  *windef;\n\tJoinExpr   *jexpr;\n\tIndexElem  *ielem;\n\tStatsElem  *selem;\n\tAlias\t   *alias;\n\tRangeVar   *range;\n\tIntoClause *into;\n\tWithClause *with;\n\tInferClause\t*infer;\n\tOnConflictClause *onconflict;\n\tA_Indices  *aind;\n\tResTarget  *target;\n\tstruct PrivTarget *privtarget;\n\tAccessPriv *accesspriv;\n\tstruct ImportQual *importqual;\n\tInsertStmt *istmt;\n\tVariableSetStmt *vsetstmt;\n\tPartitionElem *partelem;\n\tPartitionSpec *partspec;\n\tPartitionBoundSpec *partboundspec;\n\tRoleSpec   *rolespec;\n\tPublicationObjSpec *publicationobjectspec;\n\tstruct SelectLimit *selectlimit;\n\tSetQuantifier setquantifier;\n\tstruct GroupClause *groupclause;\n\tMergeMatchKind mergematch;\n\tMergeWhenClause *mergewhen;\n\tstruct KeyActions *keyactions;\n\tstruct KeyAction *keyaction;\n\tReturningClause *retclause;\n\tReturningOptionKind retoptionkind;\n}\n\n%type <node>\tstmt toplevel_stmt schema_stmt routine_body_stmt\n\t\tAlterEventTrigStmt AlterCollationStmt\n\t\tAlterDatabaseStmt AlterDatabaseSetStmt AlterDomainStmt AlterEnumStmt\n\t\tAlterFdwStmt AlterForeignServerStmt AlterGroupStmt\n\t\tAlterObjectDependsStmt AlterObjectSchemaStmt AlterOwnerStmt\n\t\tAlterOperatorStmt AlterTypeStmt AlterSeqStmt AlterSystemStmt over_clause window_specification\n\t\t\t\topt_frame_clause frame_extent frame_bound\n%type <ival>\topt_window_exclusion_clause\n%type <str>\t\topt_existing_window_name\n%type <boolean> opt_if_not_exists\n%type <boolean> opt_unique_null_treatment\n%type <ival>\tgenerated_when override_kind opt_virtual_or_stored\n%type <partspec>\tPartitionSpec OptPartitionSpec\n%type <partelem>\tpart_elem\n%type <list>\t\tpart_params\n%type <partboundspec> PartitionBoundSpec\n%type <list>\t\thash_partbound\n%type <defelt>\t\thash_partbound_elem\n\n%type <node>\tjson_format_clause\n\t\t\t\tjson_format_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_value_expr\n\t\t\t\tjson_returning_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\tjson_aggregate_func\n\t\t\t\tjson_argument\n\t\t\t\tjson_behavior\n\t\t\t\tjson_on_error_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_table\n\t\t\t\tjson_table_column_definition\n\t\t\t\tjson_table_column_path_clause_opt\n%type <list>\tjson_name_and_value_list\n\t\t\t\tjson_value_expr_list\n\t\t\t\tjson_array_aggregate_order_by_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_arguments\n\t\t\t\tjson_behavior_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_passing_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_table_column_definition_list\n%type <str>\t\tjson_table_path_name_opt\n%type <ival>\tjson_behavior_type\n\t\t\t\tjson_predicate_type_constraint\n\t\t\t\tjson_quotes_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_wrapper_behavior\n%type <boolean>\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_object_constructor_null_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_array_constructor_null_clause_opt\n\n\n/*\n * Non-keyword token types.  These are hard-wired into the \"flex\" lexer.\n * They must be listed first so that their numeric codes do not depend on\n * the set of keywords.  PL/pgSQL depends on this so that it can share the\n * same lexer.  If you add/change tokens here, fix PL/pgSQL to match!\n *\n * UIDENT and USCONST are reduced to IDENT and SCONST in parser.c, so that\n * they need no productions here; but we must assign token codes to them.\n *\n * DOT_DOT is unused in the core SQL grammar, and so will always provoke\n * parse errors.  It is needed by PL/pgSQL.\n */\n%token <str>\tIDENT UIDENT FCONST SCONST USCONST BCONST XCONST Op\n%token <ival>\tICONST PARAM\n%token\t\t\tTYPECAST DOT_DOT COLON_EQUALS EQUALS_GREATER\n%token\t\t\tLESS_EQUALS GREATER_EQUALS NOT_EQUALS\n\n/*\n * If you want to make any keyword changes, update the keyword table in\n * src/include/parser/kwlist.h and add new keywords to the appropriate one\n * of the reserved-or-not-so-reserved keyword lists, below; search\n * this file for \"Keyword category lists\".\n */\n\n/* ordinary key words in alphabetical order */\n%token <keyword> ABORT_P ABSENT ABSOLUTE_P ACCESS ACTION ADD_P ADMIN AFTER\n\tAGGREGATE ALL ALSO ALTER ALWAYS ANALYSE ANALYZE AND ANY ARRAY AS ASC\n\tASENSITIVE ASSERTION ASSIGNMENT ASYMMETRIC ATOMIC AT ATTACH ATTRIBUTE AUTHORIZATION\n\n\tBACKWARD BEFORE BEGIN_P BETWEEN BIGINT BINARY BIT\n\tBOOLEAN_P BOTH BREADTH BY\n\n\tCACHE CALL CALLED CASCADE CASCADED CASE CAST CATALOG_P CHAIN CHAR_P\n\tCHARACTER CHARACTERISTICS CHECK CHECKPOINT CLASS CLOSE\n\tCLUSTER COALESCE COLLATE COLLATION COLUMN COLUMNS COMMENT COMMENTS COMMIT\n\tCOMMITTED COMPRESSION CONCURRENTLY CONDITIONAL CONFIGURATION CONFLICT\n\tCONNECTION CONSTRAINT CONSTRAINTS CONTENT_P CONTINUE_P CONVERSION_P COPY\n\tCOST CREATE CROSS CSV CUBE CURRENT_P\n\tCURRENT_CATALOG CURRENT_DATE CURRENT_ROLE CURRENT_SCHEMA\n\tCURRENT_TIME CURRENT_TIMESTAMP CURRENT_USER CURSOR CYCLE\n\n\tDATA_P DATABASE DAY_P DEALLOCATE DEC DECIMAL_P DECLARE DEFAULT DEFAULTS\n\tDEFERRABLE DEFERRED DEFINER DELETE_P DELIMITER DELIMITERS DEPENDS DEPTH DESC\n\tDETACH DICTIONARY DISABLE_P DISCARD DISTINCT DO DOCUMENT_P DOMAIN_P\n\tDOUBLE_P DROP\n\n\tEACH ELSE EMPTY_P ENABLE_P ENCODING ENCRYPTED END_P ENFORCED ENUM_P ERROR_P\n\tESCAPE EVENT EXCEPT EXCLUDE EXCLUDING EXCLUSIVE EXECUTE EXISTS EXPLAIN\n\tEXPRESSION EXTENSION EXTERNAL EXTRACT\n\n\tFALSE_P FAMILY FETCH FILTER FINALIZE FIRST_P FLOAT_P FOLLOWING FOR\n\tFORCE FOREIGN FORMAT FORWARD FREEZE FROM FULL FUNCTION FUNCTIONS\n\n\tGENERATED GLOBAL GRANT GRANTED GREATEST GROUP_P GROUPING GROUPS\n\n\tHANDLER HAVING HEADER_P HOLD HOUR_P\n\n\tIDENTITY_P IF_P ILIKE IMMEDIATE IMMUTABLE IMPLICIT_P IMPORT_P IN_P INCLUDE\n\tINCLUDING INCREMENT INDENT INDEX INDEXES INHERIT INHERITS INITIALLY INLINE_P\n\tINNER_P INOUT INPUT_P INSENSITIVE INSERT INSTEAD INT_P INTEGER\n\tINTERSECT INTERVAL INTO INVOKER IS ISNULL ISOLATION\n\n\tJOIN JSON JSON_ARRAY JSON_ARRAYAGG JSON_EXISTS JSON_OBJECT JSON_OBJECTAGG\n\tJSON_QUERY JSON_SCALAR JSON_SERIALIZE JSON_TABLE JSON_VALUE\n\n\tKEEP KEY KEYS\n\n\tLABEL LANGUAGE LARGE_P LAST_P LATERAL_P\n\tLEADING LEAKPROOF LEAST LEFT LEVEL LIKE LIMIT LISTEN LOAD LOCAL\n\tLOCALTIME LOCALTIMESTAMP LOCATION LOCK_P LOCKED LOGGED\n\n\tMAPPING MATCH MATCHED MATERIALIZED MAXVALUE MERGE MERGE_ACTION METHOD\n\tMINUTE_P MINVALUE MODE MONTH_P MOVE\n\n\tNAME_P NAMES NATIONAL NATURAL NCHAR NESTED NEW NEXT NFC NFD NFKC NFKD NO\n\tNONE NORMALIZE NORMALIZED\n\tNOT NOTHING NOTIFY NOTNULL NOWAIT NULL_P NULLIF\n\tNULLS_P NUMERIC\n\n\tOBJECT_P OBJECTS_P OF OFF OFFSET OIDS OLD OMIT ON ONLY OPERATOR OPTION OPTIONS OR\n\tORDER ORDINALITY OTHERS OUT_P OUTER_P\n\tOVER OVERLAPS OVERLAY OVERRIDING OWNED OWNER\n\n\tPARALLEL PARAMETER PARSER PARTIAL PARTITION PASSING PASSWORD PATH\n\tPERIOD PLACING PLAN PLANS POLICY\n\tPOSITION PRECEDING PRECISION PRESERVE PREPARE PREPARED PRIMARY\n\tPRIOR PRIVILEGES PROCEDURAL PROCEDURE PROCEDURES PROGRAM PUBLICATION\n\n\tQUOTE QUOTES\n\n\tRANGE READ REAL REASSIGN RECURSIVE REF_P REFERENCES REFERENCING\n\tREFRESH REINDEX RELATIVE_P RELEASE RENAME REPEATABLE REPLACE REPLICA\n\tRESET RESTART RESTRICT RETURN RETURNING RETURNS REVOKE RIGHT ROLE ROLLBACK ROLLUP\n\tROUTINE ROUTINES ROW ROWS RULE\n\n\tSAVEPOINT SCALAR SCHEMA SCHEMAS SCROLL SEARCH SECOND_P SECURITY SELECT\n\tSEQUENCE SEQUENCES\n\tSERIALIZABLE SERVER SESSION SESSION_USER SET SETS SETOF SHARE SHOW\n\tSIMILAR SIMPLE SKIP SMALLINT SNAPSHOT SOME SOURCE SQL_P STABLE STANDALONE_P\n\tSTART STATEMENT STATISTICS STDIN STDOUT STORAGE STORED STRICT_P STRING_P STRIP_P\n\tSUBSCRIPTION SUBSTRING SUPPORT SYMMETRIC SYSID SYSTEM_P SYSTEM_USER\n\n\tTABLE TABLES TABLESAMPLE TABLESPACE TARGET TEMP TEMPLATE TEMPORARY TEXT_P THEN\n\tTIES TIME TIMESTAMP TO TRAILING TRANSACTION TRANSFORM\n\tTREAT TRIGGER TRIM TRUE_P\n\tTRUNCATE TRUSTED TYPE_P TYPES_P\n\n\tUESCAPE UNBOUNDED UNCONDITIONAL UNCOMMITTED UNENCRYPTED UNION UNIQUE UNKNOWN\n\tUNLISTEN UNLOGGED UNTIL UPDATE USER USING\n\n\tVACUUM VALID VALIDATE VALIDATOR VALUE_P VALUES VARCHAR VARIADIC VARYING\n\tVERBOSE VERSION_P VIEW VIEWS VIRTUAL VOLATILE\n\n\tWHEN WHERE WHITESPACE_P WINDOW WITH WITHIN WITHOUT WORK WRAPPER WRITE\n\n\tXML_P XMLATTRIBUTES XMLCONCAT XMLELEMENT XMLEXISTS XMLFOREST XMLNAMESPACES\n\tXMLPARSE XMLPI XMLROOT XMLSERIALIZE XMLTABLE\n\n\tYEAR_P YES_P\n\n\tZONE\n\n/*\n * The grammar thinks these are keywords, but they are not in the kwlist.h\n * list and so can never be entered directly.  The filter in parser.c\n * creates these tokens when required (based on looking one token ahead).\n *\n * NOT_LA exists so that productions such as NOT LIKE can be given the same\n * precedence as LIKE; otherwise they'd effectively have the same precedence\n * as NOT, at least with respect to their left-hand subexpression.\n * FORMAT_LA, NULLS_LA, WITH_LA, and WITHOUT_LA are needed to make the grammar\n * LALR(1).\n */\n%token\t\tFORMAT_LA NOT_LA NULLS_LA WITH_LA WITHOUT_LA\n\n/*\n * The grammar likewise thinks these tokens are keywords, but they are never\n * generated by the scanner.  Rather, they can be injected by parser.c as\n * the initial token of the string (using the lookahead-token mechanism\n * implemented there).  This provides a way to tell the grammar to parse\n * something other than the usual list of SQL commands.\n */\n%token\t\tMODE_TYPE_NAME\n%token\t\tMODE_PLPGSQL_EXPR\n%token\t\tMODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN1\n%token\t\tMODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN2\n%token\t\tMODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN3\n\n\n/* Precedence: lowest to highest */\n%left\t\tUNION EXCEPT\n%left\t\tINTERSECT\n%left\t\tOR\n%left\t\tAND\n%right\t\tNOT\n%nonassoc\tIS ISNULL NOTNULL\t/* IS sets precedence for IS NULL, etc */\n%nonassoc\t'<' '>' '=' LESS_EQUALS GREATER_EQUALS NOT_EQUALS\n%nonassoc\tBETWEEN IN_P LIKE ILIKE SIMILAR NOT_LA\n%nonassoc\tESCAPE\t\t\t/* ESCAPE must be just above LIKE/ILIKE/SIMILAR */\n\n/*\n * Sometimes it is necessary to assign precedence to keywords that are not\n * really part of the operator hierarchy, in order to resolve grammar\n * ambiguities.  It's best to avoid doing so whenever possible, because such\n * assignments have global effect and may hide ambiguities besides the one\n * you intended to solve.  (Attaching a precedence to a single rule with\n * %prec is far safer and should be preferred.)  If you must give precedence\n * to a new keyword, try very hard to give it the same precedence as IDENT.\n * If the keyword has IDENT's precedence then it clearly acts the same as\n * non-keywords and other similar keywords, thus reducing the risk of\n * unexpected precedence effects.\n *\n * We used to need to assign IDENT an explicit precedence just less than Op,\n * to support target_el without AS.  While that's not really necessary since\n * we removed postfix operators, we continue to do so because it provides a\n * reference point for a precedence level that we can assign to other\n * keywords that lack a natural precedence level.\n *\n * We need to do this for PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS, and GROUPS to support\n * opt_existing_window_name (see comment there).\n *\n * The frame_bound productions UNBOUNDED PRECEDING and UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\n * are even messier: since UNBOUNDED is an unreserved keyword (per spec!),\n * there is no principled way to distinguish these from the productions\n * a_expr PRECEDING/FOLLOWING.  We hack this up by giving UNBOUNDED slightly\n * lower precedence than PRECEDING and FOLLOWING.  At present this doesn't\n * appear to cause UNBOUNDED to be treated differently from other unreserved\n * keywords anywhere else in the grammar, but it's definitely risky.  We can\n * blame any funny behavior of UNBOUNDED on the SQL standard, though.\n *\n * To support CUBE and ROLLUP in GROUP BY without reserving them, we give them\n * an explicit priority lower than '(', so that a rule with CUBE '(' will shift\n * rather than reducing a conflicting rule that takes CUBE as a function name.\n * Using the same precedence as IDENT seems right for the reasons given above.\n *\n * SET is likewise assigned the same precedence as IDENT, to support the\n * relation_expr_opt_alias production (see comment there).\n *\n * KEYS, OBJECT_P, SCALAR, VALUE_P, WITH, and WITHOUT are similarly assigned\n * the same precedence as IDENT.  This allows resolving conflicts in the\n * json_predicate_type_constraint and json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\n * productions (see comments there).\n *\n * Like the UNBOUNDED PRECEDING/FOLLOWING case, NESTED is assigned a lower\n * precedence than PATH to fix ambiguity in the json_table production.\n */\n%nonassoc\tUNBOUNDED NESTED /* ideally would have same precedence as IDENT */\n%nonassoc\tIDENT PARTITION RANGE ROWS GROUPS PRECEDING FOLLOWING CUBE ROLLUP\n\t\t\tSET KEYS OBJECT_P SCALAR VALUE_P WITH WITHOUT PATH\n%left\t\tOp OPERATOR\t\t/* multi-character ops and user-defined operators */\n%left\t\t'+' '-'\n%left\t\t'*' '/' '%'\n%left\t\t'^'\n/* Unary Operators */\n%left\t\tAT\t\t\t\t/* sets precedence for AT TIME ZONE, AT LOCAL */\n%left\t\tCOLLATE\n%right\t\tUMINUS\n%left\t\t'[' ']'\n%left\t\t'(' ')'\n%left\t\tTYPECAST\n%left\t\t'.'\n/*\n * These might seem to be low-precedence, but actually they are not part\n * of the arithmetic hierarchy at all in their use as JOIN operators.\n * We make them high-precedence to support their use as function names.\n * They wouldn't be given a precedence at all, were it not that we need\n * left-associativity among the JOIN rules themselves.\n */\n%left\t\tJOIN CROSS LEFT FULL RIGHT INNER_P NATURAL\n\n%%\n\n/*\n *\tThe target production for the whole parse.\n *\n * Ordinarily we parse a list of statements, but if we see one of the\n * special MODE_XXX symbols as first token, we parse something else.\n * The options here correspond to enum RawParseMode, which see for details.\n */\nparse_toplevel:\n\t\t\tstmtmulti\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree = $1;\n\t\t\t\t(void) yynerrs;\t\t/* suppress compiler warning */\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| MODE_TYPE_NAME Typename\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree = list_make1($2);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| MODE_PLPGSQL_EXPR PLpgSQL_Expr\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree =\n\t\t\t\t\tlist_make1(makeRawStmt($2, @2));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| MODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN1 PLAssignStmt\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tPLAssignStmt *n = (PLAssignStmt *) $2;\n\n\t\t\t\tn->nnames = 1;\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree =\n\t\t\t\t\tlist_make1(makeRawStmt((Node *) n, @2));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| MODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN2 PLAssignStmt\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tPLAssignStmt *n = (PLAssignStmt *) $2;\n\n\t\t\t\tn->nnames = 2;\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree =\n\t\t\t\t\tlist_make1(makeRawStmt((Node *) n, @2));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| MODE_PLPGSQL_ASSIGN3 PLAssignStmt\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tPLAssignStmt *n = (PLAssignStmt *) $2;\n\n\t\t\t\tn->nnames = 3;\n\t\t\t\tpg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree =\n\t\t\t\t\tlist_make1(makeRawStmt((Node *) n, @2));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * At top level, we wrap each stmt with a RawStmt node carrying start location\n * and length of the stmt's text.\n * We also take care to discard empty statements entirely (which among other\n * things dodges the problem of assigning them a location).\n */\nstmtmulti:\tstmtmulti ';' toplevel_stmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($1 != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t/* update length of previous stmt */\n\t\t\t\t\t\tupdateRawStmtEnd(llast_node(RawStmt, $1), @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($3 != NULL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, makeRawStmt($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| toplevel_stmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($1 != NULL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1(makeRawStmt($1, @1));\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * toplevel_stmt includes BEGIN and END.  stmt does not include them, because\n * those words have different meanings in function bodies.\n */\ntoplevel_stmt:\n\t\t\tstmt\n\t\t\t| TransactionStmtLegacy\n\t\t;\n\nstmt:\n\t\t\tAlterEventTrigStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterCollationStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterDatabaseStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterDatabaseSetStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterDefaultPrivilegesStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterDomainStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterEnumStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterExtensionStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterExtensionContentsStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterFdwStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterForeignServerStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterFunctionStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterGroupStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterObjectDependsStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterObjectSchemaStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterOwnerStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterOperatorStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterTypeStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterPolicyStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterSeqStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterSystemStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterTableStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterTblSpcStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterCompositeTypeStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterPublicationStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterRoleSetStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterRoleStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterSubscriptionStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterStatsStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterTSConfigurationStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterTSDictionaryStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterUserMappingStmt\n\t\t\t| AnalyzeStmt\n\t\t\t| CallStmt\n\t\t\t| CheckPointStmt\n\t\t\t| ClosePortalStmt\n\t\t\t| ClusterStmt\n\t\t\t| CommentStmt\n\t\t\t| ConstraintsSetStmt\n\t\t\t| CopyStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateAmStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateAsStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateAssertionStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateCastStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateConversionStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateDomainStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateExtensionStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateFdwStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateForeignServerStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateForeignTableStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateFunctionStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateGroupStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateMatViewStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateOpClassStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateOpFamilyStmt\n\t\t\t| CreatePublicationStmt\n\t\t\t| AlterOpFamilyStmt\n\t\t\t| CreatePolicyStmt\n\t\t\t| CreatePLangStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateSchemaStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateSeqStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateSubscriptionStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateStatsStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateTableSpaceStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateTransformStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateTrigStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateEventTrigStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateRoleStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateUserStmt\n\t\t\t| CreateUserMappingStmt\n\t\t\t| CreatedbStmt\n\t\t\t| DeallocateStmt\n\t\t\t| DeclareCursorStmt\n\t\t\t| DefineStmt\n\t\t\t| DeleteStmt\n\t\t\t| DiscardStmt\n\t\t\t| DoStmt\n\t\t\t| DropCastStmt\n\t\t\t| DropOpClassStmt\n\t\t\t| DropOpFamilyStmt\n\t\t\t| DropOwnedStmt\n\t\t\t| DropStmt\n\t\t\t| DropSubscriptionStmt\n\t\t\t| DropTableSpaceStmt\n\t\t\t| DropTransformStmt\n\t\t\t| DropRoleStmt\n\t\t\t| DropUserMappingStmt\n\t\t\t| DropdbStmt\n\t\t\t| ExecuteStmt\n\t\t\t| ExplainStmt\n\t\t\t| FetchStmt\n\t\t\t| GrantStmt\n\t\t\t| GrantRoleStmt\n\t\t\t| ImportForeignSchemaStmt\n\t\t\t| IndexStmt\n\t\t\t| InsertStmt\n\t\t\t| ListenStmt\n\t\t\t| RefreshMatViewStmt\n\t\t\t| LoadStmt\n\t\t\t| LockStmt\n\t\t\t| MergeStmt\n\t\t\t| NotifyStmt\n\t\t\t| PrepareStmt\n\t\t\t| ReassignOwnedStmt\n\t\t\t| ReindexStmt\n\t\t\t| RemoveAggrStmt\n\t\t\t| RemoveFuncStmt\n\t\t\t| RemoveOperStmt\n\t\t\t| RenameStmt\n\t\t\t| RevokeStmt\n\t\t\t| RevokeRoleStmt\n\t\t\t| RuleStmt\n\t\t\t| SecLabelStmt\n\t\t\t| SelectStmt\n\t\t\t| TransactionStmt\n\t\t\t| TruncateStmt\n\t\t\t| UnlistenStmt\n\t\t\t| UpdateStmt\n\t\t\t| VacuumStmt\n\t\t\t| VariableResetStmt\n\t\t\t| VariableSetStmt\n\t\t\t| VariableShowStmt\n\t\t\t| ViewStmt\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Generic supporting productions for DDL\n */\nopt_single_name:\n\t\t\tColId\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_qualified_name:\n\t\t\tany_name\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_concurrently:\n\t\t\tCONCURRENTLY\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_drop_behavior:\n\t\t\tCASCADE\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = DROP_CASCADE; }\n\t\t\t| RESTRICT\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = DROP_RESTRICT; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = DROP_RESTRICT; /* default */ }\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * CALL statement\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nCallStmt:\tCALL func_application\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCallStmt   *n = makeNode(CallStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->funccall = castNode(FuncCall, $2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * Create a new Postgres DBMS role\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nCreateRoleStmt:\n\t\t\tCREATE ROLE RoleId opt_with OptRoleList\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCreateRoleStmt *n = makeNode(CreateRoleStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->stmt_type = ROLESTMT_ROLE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->role = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\nopt_with:\tWITH\n\t\t\t| WITH_LA\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Options for CREATE ROLE and ALTER ROLE (also used by CREATE/ALTER USER\n * for backwards compatibility).  Note: the only option required by SQL99\n * is \"WITH ADMIN name\".\n */\nOptRoleList:\n\t\t\tOptRoleList CreateOptRoleElem\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nAlterOptRoleList:\n\t\t\tAlterOptRoleList AlterOptRoleElem\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nAlterOptRoleElem:\n\t\t\tPASSWORD Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"password\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t (Node *) makeString($2), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| PASSWORD NULL_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"password\", NULL, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ENCRYPTED PASSWORD Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * These days, passwords are always stored in encrypted\n\t\t\t\t\t * form, so there is no difference between PASSWORD and\n\t\t\t\t\t * ENCRYPTED PASSWORD.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"password\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t (Node *) makeString($3), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| UNENCRYPTED PASSWORD Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"UNENCRYPTED PASSWORD is no longer supported\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Remove UNENCRYPTED to store the password in encrypted form instead.\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INHERIT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"inherit\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CONNECTION LIMIT SignedIconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"connectionlimit\", (Node *) makeInteger($3), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| VALID UNTIL Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"validUntil\", (Node *) makeString($3), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t/*\tSupported but not documented for roles, for use by ALTER GROUP. */\n\t\t\t| USER role_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"rolemembers\", (Node *) $2, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| IDENT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * We handle identifiers that aren't parser keywords with\n\t\t\t\t\t * the following special-case codes, to avoid bloating the\n\t\t\t\t\t * size of the main parser.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp($1, \"superuser\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"superuser\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"nosuperuser\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"superuser\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"createrole\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"createrole\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"nocreaterole\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"createrole\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"replication\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"isreplication\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"noreplication\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"isreplication\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"createdb\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"createdb\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"nocreatedb\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"createdb\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"login\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"canlogin\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"nologin\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"canlogin\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"bypassrls\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"bypassrls\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"nobypassrls\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"bypassrls\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"noinherit\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t\t * Note that INHERIT is a keyword, so it's handled by main parser, but\n\t\t\t\t\t\t * NOINHERIT is handled here.\n\t\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"inherit\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"unrecognized role option \\\"%s\\\"\", $1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nCreateOptRoleElem:\n\t\t\tAlterOptRoleElem\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t/* The following are not supported by ALTER ROLE/USER/GROUP */\n\t\t\t| SYSID Iconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"sysid\", (Node *) makeInteger($2), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ADMIN role_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"adminmembers\", (Node *) $2, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ROLE role_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem(\"rolemembers\", (Node *) $2, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| IN_P ROLE role_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * Create a new Postgres DBMS user (role with implied login ability)\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nCreateUserStmt:\n\t\t\tCREATE USER RoleId opt_with OptRoleList\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCreateRoleStmt *n = makeNode(CreateRoleStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->stmt_type = ROLESTMT_USER;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->role = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * Alter a postgresql DBMS role\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nAlterRoleStmt:\n\t\t\tALTER ROLE RoleSpec opt_with AlterOptRoleList\n\t\t\t\t {\n\t\t\t\t\tAlterRoleStmt *n = makeNode(AlterRoleStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->role = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->action = +1;\t/* add, if there are members */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t }\n\t\t\t| ALTER USER RoleSpec opt_with AlterOptRoleList\n\t\t\t\t {\n\t\t\t\t\tAlterRoleStmt *n = makeNode(AlterRoleStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->role = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->action = +1;\t/* add, if there are members */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_in_database:\n\t\t\t   /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t\t| IN_P DATABASE name\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nAlterRoleSetStmt:\n\t\t\tALTER ROLE RoleSpec opt_in_database SetResetClause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlterRoleSetStmt *n = makeNode(AlterRoleSetStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->role = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->database = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->setstmt = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ALTER ROLE ALL opt_in_database SetResetClause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlterRoleSetStmt *n =
