SystemTypeName(\"json\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\texpression grammar\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/*\n * General expressions\n * This is the heart of the expression syntax.\n *\n * We have two expression types: a_expr is the unrestricted kind, and\n * b_expr is a subset that must be used in some places to avoid shift/reduce\n * conflicts.  For example, we can't do BETWEEN as \"BETWEEN a_expr AND a_expr\"\n * because that use of AND conflicts with AND as a boolean operator.  So,\n * b_expr is used in BETWEEN and we remove boolean keywords from b_expr.\n *\n * Note that '(' a_expr ')' is a b_expr, so an unrestricted expression can\n * always be used by surrounding it with parens.\n *\n * c_expr is all the productions that are common to a_expr and b_expr;\n * it's factored out just to eliminate redundant coding.\n *\n * Be careful of productions involving more than one terminal token.\n * By default, bison will assign such productions the precedence of their\n * last terminal, but in nearly all cases you want it to be the precedence\n * of the first terminal instead; otherwise you will not get the behavior\n * you expect!  So we use %prec annotations freely to set precedences.\n */\na_expr:\t\tc_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| a_expr TYPECAST Typename\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr COLLATE any_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCollateClause *n = makeNode(CollateClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->collname = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr AT TIME ZONE a_expr\t\t\t%prec AT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"timezone\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make2($5, $1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr AT LOCAL\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec AT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"timezone\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   -1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t/*\n\t\t * These operators must be called out explicitly in order to make use\n\t\t * of bison's automatic operator-precedence handling.  All other\n\t\t * operator names are handled by the generic productions using \"Op\",\n\t\t * below; and all those operators will have the same precedence.\n\t\t *\n\t\t * If you add more explicitly-known operators, be sure to add them\n\t\t * also to b_expr and to the MathOp list below.\n\t\t */\n\t\t\t| '+' a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| '-' a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '+' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '-' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"-\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '*' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"*\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '/' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"/\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '%' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"%\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '^' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"^\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '<' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '>' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '=' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr LESS_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr GREATER_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<>\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr qual_Op a_expr\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| qual_Op a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr AND a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeAndExpr($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr OR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeOrExpr($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| NOT a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr LIKE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $3, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec LIKE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"like_escape\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make2($3, $5),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"!~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $4, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"like_escape\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make2($4, $6),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"!~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr ILIKE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, \"~~*\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $3, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr ILIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"like_escape\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make2($3, $5),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, \"~~*\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA ILIKE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) then transforming the target expressions\n\t\t\t\t\t * from whatever they are into count(*), and testing the\n\t\t\t\t\t * entire result equal to one.\n\t\t\t\t\t * But, will probably implement a separate node in the executor.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"UNIQUE predicate is not yet makeNotExpr(makeJsonIsPredicate($1, $2, $5, $6, @1), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t*/\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * The SQL spec only allows DEFAULT in \"contextually typed\n\t\t\t\t\t * expressions\", but for us, it's easier to allow it in\n\t\t\t\t\t * any a_expr and then throw error during parse analysis\n\t\t\t\t\t * if it's in an inappropriate context.  This way also\n\t\t\t\t\t * lets us say something smarter than \"syntax error\".\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tSetToDefault *n = makeNode(SetToDefault);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* parse analysis will fill in the rest */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Restricted expressions\n *\n * b_expr is a subset of the complete expression syntax defined by a_expr.\n *\n * Presently, AND, NOT, IS, and IN are the a_expr keywords that would\n * cause trouble in the places where b_expr is used.  For simplicity, we\n * just eliminate all the boolean-keyword-operator productions from b_expr.\n */\nb_expr:\t\tc_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| b_expr TYPECAST Typename\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| '+' b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| '-' b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '+' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '-' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"-\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '*' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"*\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '/' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"/\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '%' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"%\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '^' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"^\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '<' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '>' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '=' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr LESS_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr GREATER_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr NOT_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<>\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr qual_Op b_expr\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| qual_Op b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS DISTINCT FROM b_expr\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $5, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS NOT DISTINCT FROM b_expr\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $6, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS NOT DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr(makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Productions that can be used in both a_expr and b_expr.\n *\n * Note: productions that refer recursively to a_expr or b_expr mostly\n * cannot appear here.\tHowever, it's OK to refer to a_exprs that occur\n * inside parentheses, such as function arguments; that cannot introduce\n * ambiguity to the b_expr syntax.\n */\nc_expr:\t\tcolumnref\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| AexprConst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| PARAM opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tParamRef   *p = makeNode(ParamRef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tp->number = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tp->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Node *) p;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) p;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($4)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($4, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| case_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| func_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Because the select_with_parens nonterminal is designed\n\t\t\t\t\t * to \"eat\" as many levels of parens as possible, the\n\t\t\t\t\t * '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection production above will\n\t\t\t\t\t * fail to match a sub-SELECT with indirection decoration;\n\t\t\t\t\t * the sub-SELECT won't be regarded as an a_expr as long\n\t\t\t\t\t * as there are parens around it.  To support applying\n\t\t\t\t\t * subscripting or field selection to a sub-SELECT result,\n\t\t\t\t\t * we need this redundant-looking production.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *a = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\ta->arg = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\ta->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) a;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| EXISTS select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXISTS_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ARRAY select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = ARRAY_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ARRAY array_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_ArrayExpr *n = castNode(A_ArrayExpr, $2);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* point outermost A_ArrayExpr to the ARRAY keyword */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| explicit_row\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRowExpr\t   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tr->args = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_typeid = InvalidOid;\t/* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->colnames = NIL;\t/* to be filled in during analysis */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_format = COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL; /* abuse */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) r;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| implicit_row\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRowExpr\t   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tr->args = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_typeid = InvalidOid;\t/* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->colnames = NIL;\t/* to be filled in during analysis */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_format = COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST; /* abuse */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) r;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GROUPING '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t  {\n\t\t\t\t  GroupingFunc *g = makeNode(GroupingFunc);\n\n\t\t\t\t  g->args = $3;\n\t\t\t\t  g->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t  $$ = (Node *) g;\n\t\t\t  }\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_application: func_name '(' ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall($1, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, list_make1($4),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->func_variadic = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list ',' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, lappend($3, $6),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->func_variadic = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' ALL func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Ideally we'd mark the FuncCall node to indicate\n\t\t\t\t\t * \"must be an aggregate\", but there's no provision\n\t\t\t\t\t * for that in FuncCall at the moment.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' DISTINCT func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_distinct = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' '*' ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * We consider AGGREGATE(*) to invoke a parameterless\n\t\t\t\t\t * aggregate.  This does the right thing for COUNT(*),\n\t\t\t\t\t * and there are no other aggregates in SQL that accept\n\t\t\t\t\t * '*' as parameter.\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * The FuncCall node is also marked agg_star = true,\n\t\t\t\t\t * so that later processing can detect what the argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * really was.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_star = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * func_expr and its cousin func_expr_windowless are split out from c_expr just\n * so that we have classifications for \"everything that is a function call or\n * looks like one\".  This isn't very important, but it saves us having to\n * document which variants are legal in places like \"FROM function()\" or the\n * backwards-compatible functional-index syntax for CREATE INDEX.\n * (Note that many of the special SQL functions wouldn't actually make any\n * sense as functional index entries, but we ignore that consideration here.)\n */\nfunc_expr: func_application within_group_clause filter_clause over_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = (FuncCall *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * The order clause for WITHIN GROUP and the one for\n\t\t\t\t\t * plain-aggregate ORDER BY share a field, so we have to\n\t\t\t\t\t * check here that at most one is present.  We also check\n\t\t\t\t\t * for DISTINCT and VARIADIC here to give a better error\n\t\t\t\t\t * location.  Other consistency checks are deferred to\n\t\t\t\t\t * parse analysis.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2 != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->agg_order != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use multiple ORDER BY clauses with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->agg_distinct)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use DISTINCT with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->func_variadic)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use VARIADIC with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_within_group = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_filter = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->over = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| json_aggregate_func filter_clause over_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonAggConstructor *n = IsA($1, JsonObjectAgg) ?\n\t\t\t\t\t\t((JsonObjectAgg *) $1)->constructor :\n\t\t\t\t\t\t((JsonArrayAgg *) $1)->constructor;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_filter = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->over = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_expr_common_subexpr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Like func_expr but does not accept WINDOW functions directly\n * (but they can still be contained in arguments for functions etc).\n * Use this when window expressions are not allowed, where needed to\n * disambiguate the grammar (e.g. in CREATE INDEX).\n */\nfunc_expr_windowless:\n\t\t\tfunc_application\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| func_expr_common_subexpr\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| json_aggregate_func\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Special expressions that are considered to be functions.\n */\nfunc_expr_common_subexpr:\n\t\t\tCOLLATION FOR '(' a_expr ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"pg_collation_for\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($4),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_DATE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_DATE, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIME\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIME, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIME '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIME_N, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIMESTAMP_N, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIME\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIME, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIME '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIME_N, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIMESTAMP_N, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_ROLE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_ROLE, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| casting.\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * Convert SystemTypeName() to SystemFuncName() even though\n\t\t\t\t\t * at the moment they result in the same thing.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) */\nxml_root_version: VERSION_P a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| VERSION_P NO VALUE_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeNullAConst(-1); }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_xml_root_standalone: ',' STANDALONE_P YES_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_NO_VALUE, -1); }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_OMITTED, -1); }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_attributes: XMLATTRIBUTES '(' xml_attribute_list ')'\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_attribute_list:\txml_attribute_el\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| xml_attribute_list ',' xml_attribute_el\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_attribute_el: a_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ndocument_or_content: DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = XMLOPTION_DOCUMENT; }\n\t\t\t| CONTENT_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = XMLOPTION_CONTENT; }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_indent_option: INDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| NO INDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_whitespace_option: PRESERVE WHITESPACE_P\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| STRIP_P WHITESPACE_P\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* We allow several variants for SQL and other compatibility. */\nxmlexists_argument:\n\t\t\tPASSING c_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| PASSING c_expr xml_passing_mech\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| PASSING xml_passing_mech c_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| PASSING xml_passing_mech c_expr xml_passing_mech\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nxml_passing_mech:\n\t\t\tBY REF_P\n\t\t\t| BY VALUE_P\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Aggregate decoration clauses\n */\nwithin_group_clause:\n\t\t\tWITHIN GROUP_P '(' sort_clause ')'\t\t{ $$ = $4; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nfilter_clause:\n\t\t\tFILTER '(' WHERE a_expr ')'\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $4; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Window Definitions\n */\nwindow_clause:\n\t\t\tWINDOW window_definition_list\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nwindow_definition_list:\n\t\t\twindow_definition\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| window_definition_list ',' window_definition\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nwindow_definition:\n\t\t\tColId AS window_specification\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $3;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nover_clause: OVER window_specification\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| OVER ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->refname = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->partitionClause = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->orderClause = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nwindow_specification: '(' opt_existing_window_name opt_partition_clause\n\t\t\t\t\t\topt_sort_clause opt_frame_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->refname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->partitionClause = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->orderClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* copy relevant fields of opt_frame_clause */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = $5->frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = $5->startOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = $5->endOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * If we see PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS or GROUPS as the first token after the '('\n * of a window_specification, we want the assumption to be that there is\n * no existing_window_name; but those keywords are unreserved and so could\n * be ColIds.  We fix this by making them have the same precedence as IDENT\n * and giving the empty production here a slightly higher precedence, so\n * that the shift/reduce conflict is resolved in favor of reducing the rule.\n * These keywords are thus precluded from being an existing_window_name but\n * are not reserved for any other purpose.\n */\nopt_existing_window_name: ColId\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t%prec Op\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_partition_clause: PARTITION BY expr_list\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * For frame clauses, we return a WindowDef, but only some fields are used:\n * frameOptions, startOffset, and endOffset.\n */\nopt_frame_clause:\n\t\t\tRANGE frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_RANGE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ROWS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_ROWS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GROUPS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_GROUPS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nframe_extent: frame_bound\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* reject invalid cases */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from following row cannot end with current row\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BETWEEN frame_bound AND frame_bound\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n1 = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n2 = $4;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* form merged options */\n\t\t\t\t\tint\t\tframeOptions = n1->frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* shift converts START_ options to END_ options */\n\t\t\t\t\tframeOptions |= n2->frameOptions << 1;\n\t\t\t\t\tframeOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_BETWEEN;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* reject invalid cases */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame end cannot be UNBOUNDED PRECEDING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW) &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t(frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from current row cannot have preceding rows\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING) &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t(frameOptions & (FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW)))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from following row cannot have preceding rows\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tn1->frameOptions = frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\tn1->endOffset = n2->startOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * This is used for both frame start and frame end, with output set up on\n * the assumption it's frame start; the frame_extent productions must reject\n * invalid cases.\n */\nframe_bound:\n\t\t\tUNBOUNDED PRECEDING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_P ROW\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr PRECEDING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_PRECEDING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_window_exclusion_clause:\n\t\t\tEXCLUDE CURRENT_P ROW\t{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_CURRENT_ROW; }\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE GROUP_P\t\t{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_GROUP; }\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE TIES\t\t\t{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_TIES; }\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE NO OTHERS\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Supporting nonterminals for expressions.\n */\n\n/* Explicit row production.\n *\n * SQL99 allows an optional ROW keyword, so we can now do single-element rows\n * without conflicting with the parenthesized a_expr production.  Without the\n * ROW keyword, there must be more than one a_expr inside the parens.\n */\nrow:\t\tROW '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| ROW '(' ')'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t\t| '(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }\n\t\t;\n\nexplicit_row:\tROW '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| ROW '(' ')'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nimplicit_row:\t'(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'\t\t{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }\n\t\t;\n\nsub_type:\tANY\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t\t| SOME\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t\t| ALL\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ALL_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t;\n\nall_Op:\t\tOp\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| MathOp\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\nMathOp:\t\t '+'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"+\"; }\n\t\t\t| '-'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"-\"; }\n\t\t\t| '*'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"*\"; }\n\t\t\t| '/'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"/\"; }\n\t\t\t| '%'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"%\"; }\n\t\t\t| '^'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"^\"; }\n\t\t\t| '<'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<\"; }\n\t\t\t| '>'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \">\"; }\n\t\t\t| '='\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"=\"; }\n\t\t\t| LESS_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<=\"; }\n\t\t\t| GREATER_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \">=\"; }\n\t\t\t| NOT_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<>\"; }\n\t\t;\n\nqual_Op:\tOp\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nqual_all_Op:\n\t\t\tall_Op\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nsubquery_Op:\n\t\t\tall_Op\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| LIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"~~\")); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA LIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"!~~\")); }\n\t\t\t| ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"~~*\")); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"!~~*\")); }\n/* cannot put SIMILAR TO here, because SIMILAR TO is a hack.\n * the regular expression is preprocessed by a function (similar_to_escape),\n * and the ~ operator for posix regular expressions is used.\n *        x SIMILAR TO y     ->    x ~ similar_to_escape(y)\n * this transformation is made on the fly by the parser upwards.\n * however the SubLink structure which handles any/some/all stuff\n * is not ready for such a thing.\n */\n\t\t\t;\n\nexpr_list:\ta_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| expr_list ',' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* function arguments can have names */\nfunc_arg_list:  func_arg_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_arg_list ',' func_arg_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_arg_expr:  a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| param_name COLON_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tna->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->arg = (Expr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->argnumber = -1;\t\t/* until determined */\n\t\t\t\t\tna->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) na;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| param_name EQUALS_GREATER a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tna->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->arg = (Expr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->argnumber = -1;\t\t/* until determined */\n\t\t\t\t\tna->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) na;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_arg_list_opt:\tfunc_arg_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\ntype_list:\tTypename\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_list ',' Typename\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\narray_expr: '[' expr_list ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' array_expr_list ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr(NIL, @1, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\narray_expr_list: array_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| array_expr_list ',' array_expr\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\nextract_list:\n\t\t\textract_arg FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(makeStringConst($1, @1), $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* Allow delimited string Sconst in extract_arg as an SQL extension.\n * - thomas 2001-04-12\n */\nextract_arg:\n\t\t\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| YEAR_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"year\"; }\n\t\t\t| MONTH_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"month\"; }\n\t\t\t| DAY_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"day\"; }\n\t\t\t| HOUR_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"hour\"; }\n\t\t\t| MINUTE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"minute\"; }\n\t\t\t| SECOND_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"second\"; }\n\t\t\t| Sconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\nunicode_normal_form:\n\t\t\tNFC\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFC\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFD\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFD\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFKC\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFKC\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFKD\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFKD\"; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* OVERLAY() arguments */\noverlay_list:\n\t\t\ta_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C FOR D) is converted to overlay(A, B, C, D) */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make4($1, $3, $5, $7);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C) is converted to overlay(A, B, C) */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* position_list uses b_expr not a_expr to avoid conflict with general IN */\nposition_list:\n\t\t\tb_expr IN_P b_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2($3, $1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SUBSTRING() arguments\n *\n * Note that SQL:1999 has both\n *     text FROM int FOR int\n * and\n *     text FROM pattern FOR escape\n *\n * In the parser we map them both to a call to the substring() function and\n * rely on type resolution to pick the right one.\n *\n * In SQL:2003, the second variant was changed to\n *     text SIMILAR pattern ESCAPE escape\n * We could in theory map that to a different function internally, but\n * since we still support the SQL:1999 version, we don't.  However,\n * ruleutils.c will reverse-list the call in the newer style.\n */\nsubstr_list:\n\t\t\ta_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOR a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* not legal per SQL, but might as well allow it */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $5, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Because we aren't restricting data types here, this\n\t\t\t\t\t * syntax can end up resolving to textregexsubstr().\n\t\t\t\t\t * We've historically allowed that to happen, so continue\n\t\t\t\t\t * to accept it.  However, ruleutils.c will reverse-list\n\t\t\t\t\t * such a call in regular function call syntax.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* not legal per SQL */\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Since there are no cases where this syntax allows\n\t\t\t\t\t * a textual FOR value, we forcibly cast the argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * to int4.  The possible matches in pg_proc are\n\t\t\t\t\t * substring(text,int4) and substring(text,text),\n\t\t\t\t\t * and we don't want the parser to choose the latter,\n\t\t\t\t\t * which it is likely to do if the second argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * is unknown or doesn't have an implicit cast to int4.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, makeIntConst(1, -1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeTypeCast($3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t SystemTypeName(\"int4\"), -1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr SIMILAR a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ntrim_list:\ta_expr FROM expr_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($3, $1); }\n\t\t\t| FROM expr_list\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| expr_list\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Define SQL-style CASE clause.\n * - Full specification\n *\tCASE WHEN a = b THEN c ... ELSE d END\n * - Implicit argument\n *\tCASE a WHEN b THEN c ... ELSE d END\n */\ncase_expr:\tCASE case_arg when_clause_list case_default END_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCaseExpr   *c = makeNode(CaseExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tc->casetype = InvalidOid; /* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tc->arg = (Expr *) $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->args = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->defresult = (Expr *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) c;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nwhen_clause_list:\n\t\t\t/* There must be at least one */\n\t\t\twhen_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| when_clause_list when_clause\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nwhen_clause:\n\t\t\tWHEN a_expr THEN a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCaseWhen   *w = makeNode(CaseWhen);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tw->expr = (Expr *) $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tw->result = (Expr *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tw->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) w;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncase_default:\n\t\t\tELSE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\ncase_arg:\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\ncolumnref:\tColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeColumnRef($1, NIL, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeColumnRef($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nindirection_el:\n\t\t\t'.' attr_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeString($2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '.' '*'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeNode(A_Star);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' a_expr ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tai->is_slice = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->lidx = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->uidx = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) ai;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' opt_slice_bound ':' opt_slice_bound ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tai->is_slice = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->lidx = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->uidx = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) ai;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_slice_bound:\n\t\t\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nindirection:\n\t\t\tindirection_el\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| indirection indirection_el\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_indirection:\n\t\t\t/*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t\t| opt_indirection indirection_el\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_asymmetric: ASYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\n/* SQL/JSON support */\njson_passing_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tPASSING json_arguments\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_arguments:\n\t\t\tjson_argument\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| json_arguments ',' json_argument\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_argument:\n\t\t\tjson_value_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tJsonArgument *n = makeNode(JsonArgument);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->val = (JsonValueExpr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\tn->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* ARRAY is a noise word */\njson_wrapper_behavior:\n\t\t\t  WITHOUT WRAPPER\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT ARRAY\tWRAPPER\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }\n\t\t\t| WITH WRAPPER\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH ARRAY WRAPPER\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH CONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER\t{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNCONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH CONDITIONAL WRAPPER\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNCONDITIONAL WRAPPER\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| /* empty */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNSPEC; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_behavior:\n\t\t\tDEFAULT a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior(JSON_BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| json_behavior_type\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior($1, NULL, @1); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_behavior_type:\n\t\t\tERROR_P\t\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_ERROR; }\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_NULL; }\n\t\t\t| TRUE_P\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_TRUE; }\n\t\t\t| FALSE_P\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_FALSE; }\n\t\t\t| UNKNOWN\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_UNKNOWN; }\n\t\t\t| EMPTY_P ARRAY\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_ARRAY; }\n\t\t\t| EMPTY_P OBJECT_P\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_OBJECT; }\n\t\t\t/* non-standard, for Oracle compatibility only */\n\t\t\t| EMPTY_P\t{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_ARRAY; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_behavior_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tjson_behavior ON EMPTY_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2($1, NULL); }\n\t\t\t| json_behavior ON ERROR_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2(NULL, $1); }\n\t\t\t| json_behavior ON EMPTY_P json_behavior ON ERROR_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2($1, $4); }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2(NULL, NULL); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_on_error_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tjson_behavior ON ERROR_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_value_expr:\n\t\t\ta_expr json_format_clause_opt\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t/* formatted_expr will be set during parse-analysis. */\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeJsonValueExpr((Expr *) $1, NULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcastNode(JsonFormat, $2));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_format_clause:\n\t\t\tFORMAT_LA JSON ENCODING name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tint\t\tencoding;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!pg_strcasecmp($4, \"utf8\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tencoding = JS_ENC_UTF8;\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (!pg_strcasecmp($4, \"utf16\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tencoding = JS_ENC_UTF16;\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (!pg_strcasecmp($4, \"utf32\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tencoding = JS_ENC_UTF32;\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"unrecognized JSON encoding: %s\", $4),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_JSON, encoding, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FORMAT_LA JSON\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_JSON, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_format_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tjson_format_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_quotes_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tKEEP QUOTES ON SCALAR STRING_P\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_KEEP; }\n\t\t\t| KEEP QUOTES\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_KEEP; }\n\t\t\t| OMIT QUOTES ON SCALAR STRING_P\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }\n\t\t\t| OMIT QUOTES\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_UNSPEC; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_returning_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tRETURNING Typename json_format_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonOutput *n = makeNode(JsonOutput);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returning = makeNode(JsonReturning);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returning->format = (JsonFormat *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * We must assign the only-JSON production a precedence less than IDENT in\n * order to favor shifting over reduction when JSON is followed by VALUE_P,\n * OBJECT_P, or SCALAR.  (ARRAY doesn't need that treatment, because it's a\n * fully reserved word.)  Because json_predicate_type_constraint is always\n * followed by json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt, we also need the only-JSON\n * production to have precedence less than WITH and WITHOUT.  UNBOUNDED isn't\n * really related to this syntax, but it's a convenient choice because it\n * already has a precedence less than IDENT for other reasons.\n */\njson_predicate_type_constraint:\n\t\t\tJSON\t\t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON VALUE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON ARRAY\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ARRAY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON OBJECT_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_OBJECT; }\n\t\t\t| JSON SCALAR\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_SCALAR; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * KEYS is a noise word here.  To avoid shift/reduce conflicts, assign the\n * KEYS-less productions a precedence less than IDENT (i.e., less than KEYS).\n * This prevents reducing them when the next token is KEYS.\n */\njson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt:\n\t\t\tWITH UNIQUE KEYS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNIQUE\t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT UNIQUE KEYS\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT UNIQUE\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */ \t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_name_and_value_list:\n\t\t\tjson_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| json_name_and_value_list ',' json_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_name_and_value:\n/* Supporting this syntax seems to require major surgery\n\t\t\tKEY c_expr VALUE_P json_value_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($2, $4); }\n\t\t\t|\n*/\n\t\t\tc_expr VALUE_P json_value_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($1, $3); }\n\t\t\t|\n\t\t\ta_expr ':' json_value_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* empty means false for objects, true for arrays */\njson_object_constructor_null_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tNULL_P ON NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| ABSENT ON NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_array_constructor_null_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tNULL_P ON NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| ABSENT ON NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_value_expr_list:\n\t\t\tjson_value_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| json_value_expr_list ',' json_value_expr\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3);}\n\t\t;\n\njson_aggregate_func:\n\t\t\tJSON_OBJECTAGG '('\n\t\t\t\tjson_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\tjson_object_constructor_null_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_returning_clause_opt\n\t\t\t')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonObjectAgg *n = makeNode(JsonObjectAgg);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (JsonKeyValue *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->absent_on_null = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->unique = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor = makeNode(JsonAggConstructor);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->output = (JsonOutput *) $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->agg_order = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| JSON_ARRAYAGG '('\n\t\t\t\tjson_value_expr\n\t\t\t\tjson_array_aggregate_order_by_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_array_constructor_null_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_returning_clause_opt\n\t\t\t')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonArrayAgg *n = makeNode(JsonArrayAgg);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->absent_on_null = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor = makeNode(JsonAggConstructor);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->agg_order = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->output = (JsonOutput *) $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constructor->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_array_aggregate_order_by_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tORDER BY sortby_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\ttarget list for SELECT\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nopt_target_list: target_list\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\ntarget_list:\n\t\t\ttarget_el\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| target_list ',' target_el\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\ntarget_el:\ta_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr BareColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '*'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tColumnRef  *n = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\tNames and constants\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nqualified_name_list:\n\t\t\tqualified_name\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| qualified_name_list ',' qualified_name { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * The production for a qualified relation name has to exactly match the\n * production for a qualified func_name, because in a FROM clause we cannot\n * tell which we are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' for a\n * func_name, something else for a relation). Therefore we allow 'indirection'\n * which may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.\n */\nqualified_name:\n\t\t\tColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRangeVar(NULL, $1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nname_list:\tname\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| name_list ',' name\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, makeString($3)); }\n\t\t;\n\n\nname:\t\tColId\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nattr_name:\tColLabel\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nfile_name:\tSconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\n/*\n * The production for a qualified func_name has to exactly match the\n * production for a qualified columnref, because we cannot tell which we\n * are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' or Sconst for a func_name,\n * anything else for a columnref).  Therefore we allow 'indirection' which\n * may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.  (If we\n * ever implement SQL99-like methods, such syntax may actually become legal!)\n */\nfunc_name:\ttype_function_name\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = check_func_name(lcons(makeString($1), $2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Constants\n */\nAexprConst: Iconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeIntConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeFloatConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| XCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* This is a bit constant per SQL99:\n\t\t\t\t\t * Without Feature F511, \"BIT data type\",\n\t\t\t\t\t * a <general literal> shall not be a\n\t\t\t\t\t * <bit string literal> or a <hex string literal>.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generic type 'literal' syntax */\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')' Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generic syntax with a type modifier */\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);\n\t\t\t\t\tListCell   *lc;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * We must use func_arg_list and opt_sort_clause in the\n\t\t\t\t\t * production to avoid reduce/reduce conflicts, but we\n\t\t\t\t\t * don't actually wish to allow NamedArgExpr in this\n\t\t\t\t\t * context, nor ORDER BY.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tforeach(lc, $3)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *arg = (NamedArgExpr *) lfirst(lc);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (IsA(arg, NamedArgExpr))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"type modifier cannot have parameter name\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(arg->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($4 != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"type modifier cannot have ORDER BY\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tt->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($6, @6, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstTypename Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, $1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval Sconst opt_interval\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')' Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($5, @5, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TRUE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBoolAConst(true, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FALSE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBoolAConst(false, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNullAConst(@1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nIconst:\t\tICONST\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\nSconst:\t\tSCONST\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nSignedIconst: Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| '+' Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = + $2; }\n\t\t\t| '-' Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = - $2; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Role specifications */\nRoleId:\t\tRoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRoleSpec   *spc = (RoleSpec *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tswitch (spc->roletype)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CSTRING:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = spc->rolename;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"role name \\\"%s\\\" is reserved\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"public\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"SESSION_USER\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"CURRENT_USER\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"CURRENT_ROLE\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t;\n\nRoleSpec:\tNonReservedWord\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * \"public\" and \"none\" are not keywords, but they must\n\t\t\t\t\t * be treated specially here.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tRoleSpec   *n;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp($1, \"public\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn = (RoleSpec *) makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_PUBLIC, @1);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->roletype = ROLESPEC_PUBLIC;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"none\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"role name \\\"%s\\\" is reserved\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"none\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CSTRING, @1);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->rolename = pstrdup($1);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_ROLE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_USER\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SESSION_USER\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nrole_list:\tRoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| role_list ',' RoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * PL/pgSQL extensions\n *\n * You'd think a PL/pgSQL \"expression\" should be just an a_expr, but\n * historically it can include just about anything that can follow SELECT.\n * Therefore the returned struct is a SelectStmt.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nPLpgSQL_Expr: opt_distinct_clause opt_target_list\n\t\t\tfrom_clause where_clause\n\t\t\tgroup_clause having_clause window_clause\n\t\t\topt_sort_clause opt_select_limit opt_for_locking_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->distinctClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupClause = ($5)->list;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupDistinct = ($5)->distinct;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->havingClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->windowClause = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->sortClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($9)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = $9->limitOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = $9->limitCount;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!n->sortClause &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$9->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"WITH TIES cannot be specified without ORDER BY clause\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition($9->optionLoc)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = $9->limitOption;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lockingClause = $10;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * PL/pgSQL Assignment statement: name opt_indirection := PLpgSQL_Expr\n */\n\nPLAssignStmt: plassign_target opt_indirection plassign_equals PLpgSQL_Expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tPLAssignStmt *n = makeNode(PLAssignStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t/* nnames will be filled by calling production */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->val = (SelectStmt *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nplassign_target: ColId\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| PARAM\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = psprintf(\"$%d\", $1); }\n\t\t;\n\nplassign_equals: COLON_EQUALS\n\t\t\t| '='\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Name classification hierarchy.\n *\n * IDENT is the lexeme returned by the lexer for identifiers that match\n * no known keyword.  In most cases, we can accept certain keywords as\n * names, not only IDENTs.\tWe prefer to accept as many such keywords\n * as possible to minimize the impact of \"reserved words\" on programmers.\n * So, we divide names into several possible classes.  The classification\n * is chosen in part to make keywords acceptable as names wherever possible.\n */\n\n/* Column identifier --- names that can be column, table, etc names.\n */\nColId:\t\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Type/function identifier --- names that can be type or function names.\n */\ntype_function_name:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Any not-fully-reserved word --- these names can be, eg, role names.\n */\nNonReservedWord:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Column label --- allowed labels in \"AS\" clauses.\n * This presently includes *all* Postgres keywords.\n */\nColLabel:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| reserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Bare column label --- names that can be column labels without writing \"AS\".\n * This classification is orthogonal to the other keyword categories.\n */\nBareColLabel:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| bare_label_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Keyword category lists.  Generally, every keyword present in\n * the Postgres grammar should appear in exactly one of these lists.\n *\n * Put a new keyword into the first list that it can go into without causing\n * shift or reduce conflicts.  The earlier lists define \"less reserved\"\n * categories of keywords.\n *\n * Make sure that each keyword's category in kwlist.h matches where\n * it is listed here.  (Someday we may be able to generate these lists and\n * kwlist.h's table from one source of truth.)\n */\n\n/* \"Unreserved\" keywords --- available for use as any kind of name.\n */\nunreserved_keyword:\n\t\t\t  ABORT_P\n\t\t\t| ABSENT\n\t\t\t| ABSOLUTE_P\n\t\t\t| ACCESS\n\t\t\t| ACTION\n\t\t\t| ADD_P\n\t\t\t| ADMIN\n\t\t\t| AFTER\n\t\t\t| AGGREGATE\n\t\t\t| ALSO\n\t\t\t| ALTER\n\t\t\t| ALWAYS\n\t\t\t| ASENSITIVE\n\t\t\t| ASSERTION\n\t\t\t| ASSIGNMENT\n\t\t\t| AT\n\t\t\t| ATOMIC\n\t\t\t| ATTACH\n\t\t\t| ATTRIBUTE\n\t\t\t| BACKWARD\n\t\t\t| BEFORE\n\t\t\t| BEGIN_P\n\t\t\t| BREADTH\n\t\t\t| BY\n\t\t\t| CACHE\n\t\t\t| CALL\n\t\t\t| CALLED\n\t\t\t| CASCADE\n\t\t\t| CASCADED\n\t\t\t| CATALOG_P\n\t\t\t| CHAIN\n\t\t\t| CHARACTERISTICS\n\t\t\t| CHECKPOINT\n\t\t\t| CLASS\n\t\t\t| CLOSE\n\t\t\t| CLUSTER\n\t\t\t| COLUMNS\n\t\t\t| COMMENT\n\t\t\t| COMMENTS\n\t\t\t| COMMIT\n\t\t\t| COMMITTED\n\t\t\t| COMPRESSION\n\t\t\t| CONDITIONAL\n\t\t\t| CONFIGURATION\n\t\t\t| CONFLICT\n\t\t\t| CONNECTION\n\t\t\t| CONSTRAINTS\n\t\t\t| CONTENT_P\n\t\t\t| CONTINUE_P\n\t\t\t| CONVERSION_P\n\t\t\t| COPY\n\t\t\t| COST\n\t\t\t| CSV\n\t\t\t| CUBE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_P\n\t\t\t| CURSOR\n\t\t\t| CYCLE\n\t\t\t| DATA_P\n\t\t\t| DATABASE\n\t\t\t| DAY_P\n\t\t\t| DEALLOCATE\n\t\t\t| DECLARE\n\t\t\t| DEFAULTS\n\t\t\t| DEFERRED\n\t\t\t| DEFINER\n\t\t\t| DELETE_P\n\t\t\t| DELIMITER\n\t\t\t| DELIMITERS\n\t\t\t| DEPENDS\n\t\t\t| DEPTH\n\t\t\t| DETACH\n\t\t\t| DICTIONARY\n\t\t\t| DISABLE_P\n\t\t\t| DISCARD\n\t\t\t| DOCUMENT_P\n\t\t\t| DOMAIN_P\n\t\t\t| DOUBLE_P\n\t\t\t| DROP\n\t\t\t| EACH\n\t\t\t| EMPTY_P\n\t\t\t| ENABLE_P\n\t\t\t| ENCODING\n\t\t\t| ENCRYPTED\n\t\t\t| ENFORCED\n\t\t\t| ENUM_P\n\t\t\t| ERROR_P\n\t\t\t| ESCAPE\n\t\t\t| EVENT\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDING\n\t\t\t| EXCLUSIVE\n\t\t\t| EXECUTE\n\t\t\t| EXPLAIN\n\t\t\t| EXPRESSION\n\t\t\t| EXTENSION\n\t\t\t| EXTERNAL\n\t\t\t| FAMILY\n\t\t\t| FILTER\n\t\t\t| FINALIZE\n\t\t\t| FIRST_P\n\t\t\t| FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t| FORCE\n\t\t\t| FORMAT\n\t\t\t| FORWARD\n\t\t\t| FUNCTION\n\t\t\t| FUNCTIONS\n\t\t\t| GENERATED\n\t\t\t| GLOBAL\n\t\t\t| GRANTED\n\t\t\t| GROUPS\n\t\t\t| HANDLER\n\t\t\t| HEADER_P\n\t\t\t| HOLD\n\t\t\t| HOUR_P\n\t\t\t| IDENTITY_P\n\t\t\t| IF_P\n\t\t\t| IMMEDIATE\n\t\t\t| IMMUTABLE\n\t\t\t| IMPLICIT_P\n\t\t\t| IMPORT_P\n\t\t\t| INCLUDE\n\t\t\t| INCLUDING\n\t\t\t| INCREMENT\n\t\t\t| INDENT\n\t\t\t| INDEX\n\t\t\t| INDEXES\n\t\t\t| INHERIT\n\t\t\t| INHERITS\n\t\t\t| INLINE_P\n\t\t\t| INPUT_P\n\t\t\t| INSENSITIVE\n\t\t\t| INSERT\n\t\t\t| INSTEAD\n\t\t\t| INVOKER\n\t\t\t| ISOLATION\n\t\t\t| KEEP\n\t\t\t| KEY\n\t\t\t| KEYS\n\t\t\t| LABEL\n\t\t\t| LANGUAGE\n\t\t\t| LARGE_P\n\t\t\t| LAST_P\n\t\t\t| LEAKPROOF\n\t\t\t| LEVEL\n\t\t\t| LISTEN\n\t\t\t| LOAD\n\t\t\t| LOCAL\n\t\t\t| LOCATION\n\t\t\t| LOCK_P\n\t\t\t| LOCKED\n\t\t\t| LOGGED\n\t\t\t| MAPPING\n\t\t\t| MATCH\n\t\t\t| MATCHED\n\t\t\t| MATERIALIZED\n\t\t\t| MAXVALUE\n\t\t\t| MERGE\n\t\t\t| METHOD\n\t\t\t| MINUTE_P\n\t\t\t| MINVALUE\n\t\t\t| MODE\n\t\t\t| MONTH_P\n\t\t\t| MOVE\n\t\t\t| NAME_P\n\t\t\t| NAMES\n\t\t\t| NESTED\n\t\t\t| NEW\n\t\t\t| NEXT\n\t\t\t| NFC\n\t\t\t| NFD\n\t\t\t| NFKC\n\t\t\t| NFKD\n\t\t\t| NO\n\t\t\t| NORMALIZED\n\t\t\t| NOTHING\n\t\t\t| NOTIFY\n\t\t\t| NOWAIT\n\t\t\t| NULLS_P\n\t\t\t| OBJECT_P\n\t\t\t| OBJECTS_P\n\t\t\t| OF\n\t\t\t| OFF\n\t\t\t| OIDS\n\t\t\t| OLD\n\t\t\t| OMIT\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR\n\t\t\t| OPTION\n\t\t\t| OPTIONS\n\t\t\t| ORDINALITY\n\t\t\t| OTHERS\n\t\t\t| OVER\n\t\t\t| OVERRIDING\n\t\t\t| OWNED\n\t\t\t| OWNER\n\t\t\t| PARALLEL\n\t\t\t| PARAMETER\n\t\t\t| PARSER\n\t\t\t| PARTIAL\n\t\t\t| PARTITION\n\t\t\t| PASSING\n\t\t\t| PASSWORD\n\t\t\t| PATH\n\t\t\t| PERIOD\n\t\t\t| PLAN\n\t\t\t| PLANS\n\t\t\t| POLICY\n\t\t\t| PRECEDING\n\t\t\t| PREPARE\n\t\t\t| PREPARED\n\t\t\t| PRESERVE\n\t\t\t| PRIOR\n\t\t\t| PRIVILEGES\n\t\t\t| PROCEDURAL\n\t\t\t| PROCEDURE\n\t\t\t| PROCEDURES\n\t\t\t| PROGRAM\n\t\t\t| PUBLICATION\n\t\t\t| QUOTE\n\t\t\t| QUOTES\n\t\t\t| RANGE\n\t\t\t| READ\n\t\t\t| REASSIGN\n\t\t\t| RECURSIVE\n\t\t\t| REF_P\n\t\t\t| REFERENCING\n\t\t\t| REFRESH\n\t\t\t| REINDEX\n\t\t\t| RELATIVE_P\n\t\t\t| RELEASE\n\t\t\t| RENAME\n\t\t\t| REPEATABLE\n\t\t\t| REPLACE\n\t\t\t| REPLICA\n\t\t\t| RESET\n\t\t\t| RESTART\n\t\t\t| RESTRICT\n\t\t\t| RETURN\n\t\t\t| RETURNS\n\t\t\t| REVOKE\n\t\t\t| ROLE\n\t\t\t| ROLLBACK\n\t\t\t| ROLLUP\n\t\t\t| ROUTINE\n\t\t\t| ROUTINES\n\t\t\t| ROWS\n\t\t\t| RULE\n\t\t\t| SAVEPOINT\n\t\t\t| SCALAR\n\t\t\t| SCHEMA\n\t\t\t| SCHEMAS\n\t\t\t| SCROLL\n\t\t\t| SEARCH\n\t\t\t| SECOND_P\n\t\t\t| SECURITY\n\t\t\t| SEQUENCE\n\t\t\t| SEQUENCES\n\t\t\t| SERIALIZABLE\n\t\t\t| SERVER\n\t\t\t| SESSION\n\t\t\t| SET\n\t\t\t| SETS\n\t\t\t| SHARE\n\t\t\t| SHOW\n\t\t\t| SIMPLE\n\t\t\t| SKIP\n\t\t\t| SNAPSHOT\n\t\t\t| SOURCE\n\t\t\t| SQL_P\n\t\t\t| STABLE\n\t\t\t| STANDALONE_P\n\t\t\t| START\n\t\t\t| STATEMENT\n\t\t\t| STATISTICS\n\t\t\t| STDIN\n\t\t\t| STDOUT\n\t\t\t| STORAGE\n\t\t\t| STORED\n\t\t\t| STRICT_P\n\t\t\t| STRING_P\n\t\t\t| STRIP_P\n\t\t\t| SUBSCRIPTION\n\t\t\t| SUPPORT\n\t\t\t| SYSID\n\t\t\t| SYSTEM_P\n\t\t\t| TABLES\n\t\t\t| TABLESPACE\n\t\t\t| TARGET\n\t\t\t| TEMP\n\t\t\t| TEMPLATE\n\t\t\t| TEMPORARY\n\t\t\t| TEXT_P\n\t\t\t| TIES\n\t\t\t| TRANSACTION\n\t\t\t| TRANSFORM\n\t\t\t| TRIGGER\n\t\t\t| TRUNCATE\n\t\t\t| TRUSTED\n\t\t\t| TYPE_P\n\t\t\t| TYPES_P\n\t\t\t| UESCAPE\n\t\t\t| UNBOUNDED\n\t\t\t| UNCOMMITTED\n\t\t\t| UNCONDITIONAL\n\t\t\t| UNENCRYPTED\n\t\t\t| UNKNOWN\n\t\t\t| UNLISTEN\n\t\t\t| UNLOGGED\n\t\t\t| UNTIL\n\t\t\t| UPDATE\n\t\t\t| VACUUM\n\t\t\t| VALID\n\t\t\t| VALIDATE\n\t\t\t| VALIDATOR\n\t\t\t| VALUE_P\n\t\t\t| VARYING\n\t\t\t| VERSION_P\n\t\t\t| VIEW\n\t\t\t| VIEWS\n\t\t\t| VIRTUAL\n\t\t\t| VOLATILE\n\t\t\t| WHITESPACE_P\n\t\t\t| WITHIN\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT\n\t\t\t| WORK\n\t\t\t| WRAPPER\n\t\t\t| WRITE\n\t\t\t| XML_P\n\t\t\t| YEAR_P\n\t\t\t| YES_P\n\t\t\t| ZONE\n\t\t;\n\n/* Column identifier --- keywords that can be column, table, etc names.\n *\n * Many of these keywords will in fact be recognized as type or function\n * names too; but they have special productions for the purpose, and so\n * can't be treated as \"generic\" type or function names.\n *\n * The type names appearing here are not usable as function names\n * because they can be followed by '(' in typename productions, which\n * looks too much like a function call for an LR(1) parser.\n */\ncol_name_keyword:\n\t\t\t  BETWEEN\n\t\t\t| BIGINT\n\t\t\t| BIT\n\t\t\t| BOOLEAN_P\n\t\t\t| CHAR_P\n\t\t\t| CHARACTER\n\t\t\t| COALESCE\n\t\t\t| DEC\n\t\t\t| DECIMAL_P\n\t\t\t| EXISTS\n\t\t\t| EXTRACT\n\t\t\t| FLOAT_P\n\t\t\t| GREATEST\n\t\t\t| GROUPING\n\t\t\t| INOUT\n\t\t\t| INT_P\n\t\t\t| INTEGER\n\t\t\t| INTERVAL\n\t\t\t| JSON\n\t\t\t| JSON_ARRAY\n\t\t\t| JSON_ARRAYAGG\n\t\t\t| JSON_EXISTS\n\t\t\t| JSON_OBJECT\n\t\t\t| JSON_OBJECTAGG\n\t\t\t| JSON_QUERY\n\t\t\t| JSON_SCALAR\n\t\t\t| JSON_SERIALIZE\n\t\t\t| JSON_TABLE\n\t\t\t| JSON_VALUE\n\t\t\t| LEAST\n\t\t\t| MERGE_ACTION\n\t\t\t| NATIONAL\n\t\t\t| NCHAR\n\t\t\t| NONE\n\t\t\t| NORMALIZE\n\t\t\t| NULLIF\n\t\t\t| NUMERIC\n\t\t\t| OUT_P\n\t\t\t| OVERLAY\n\t\t\t| POSITION\n\t\t\t| PRECISION\n\t\t\t| REAL\n\t\t\t| ROW\n\t\t\t| SETOF\n\t\t\t| SMALLINT\n\t\t\t| SUBSTRING\n\t\t\t| TIME\n\t\t\t| TIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| TREAT\n\t\t\t| TRIM\n\t\t\t| VALUES\n\t\t\t| VARCHAR\n\t\t\t| XMLATTRIBUTES\n\t\t\t| XMLCONCAT\n\t\t\t| XMLELEMENT\n\t\t\t| XMLEXISTS\n\t\t\t| XMLFOREST\n\t\t\t| XMLNAMESPACES\n\t\t\t| XMLPARSE\n\t\t\t| XMLPI\n\t\t\t| XMLROOT\n\t\t\t| XMLSERIALIZE\n\t\t\t| XMLTABLE\n\t\t;\n\n/* Type/function identifier --- keywords that can be type or function names.\n *\n * Most of these are keywords that are used as operators in expressions;\n * in general such keywords can't be column names because they would be\n * ambiguous with variables, but they are unambiguous as function identifiers.\n *\n * Do not include POSITION, SUBSTRING, etc here since they have explicit\n * productions in a_expr to support the goofy SQL9x argument syntax.\n * - thomas 2000-11-28\n */\ntype_func_name_keyword:\n\t\t\t  AUTHORIZATION\n\t\t\t| BINARY\n\t\t\t| COLLATION\n\t\t\t| CONCURRENTLY\n\t\t\t| CROSS\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_SCHEMA\n\t\t\t| FREEZE\n\t\t\t| FULL\n\t\t\t| ILIKE\n\t\t\t| INNER_P\n\t\t\t| IS\n\t\t\t| ISNULL\n\t\t\t| JOIN\n\t\t\t| LEFT\n\t\t\t| LIKE\n\t\t\t| NATURAL\n\t\t\t| NOTNULL\n\t\t\t| OUTER_P\n\t\t\t| OVERLAPS\n\t\t\t| RIGHT\n\t\t\t| SIMILAR\n\t\t\t| TABLESAMPLE\n\t\t\t| VERBOSE\n\t\t;\n\n/* Reserved keyword --- these keywords are usable only as a ColLabel.\n *\n * Keywords appear here if they could not be distinguished from variable,\n * type, or function names in some contexts.  Don't put things here unless\n * forced to.\n */\nreserved_keyword:\n\t\t\t  ALL\n\t\t\t| ANALYSE\n\t\t\t| ANALYZE\n\t\t\t| AND\n\t\t\t| ANY\n\t\t\t| ARRAY\n\t\t\t| AS\n\t\t\t| ASC\n\t\t\t| ASYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| BOTH\n\t\t\t| CASE\n\t\t\t| CAST\n\t\t\t| CHECK\n\t\t\t| COLLATE\n\t\t\t| COLUMN\n\t\t\t| CONSTRAINT\n\t\t\t| CREATE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_CATALOG\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_DATE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_ROLE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIME\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_USER\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT\n\t\t\t| DEFERRABLE\n\t\t\t| DESC\n\t\t\t| DISTINCT\n\t\t\t| DO\n\t\t\t| ELSE\n\t\t\t| END_P\n\t\t\t| EXCEPT\n\t\t\t| FALSE_P\n\t\t\t| FETCH\n\t\t\t| FOR\n\t\t\t| FOREIGN\n\t\t\t| FROM\n\t\t\t| GRANT\n\t\t\t| GROUP_P\n\t\t\t| HAVING\n\t\t\t| IN_P\n\t\t\t| INITIALLY\n\t\t\t| INTERSECT\n\t\t\t| INTO\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P\n\t\t\t| LEADING\n\t\t\t| LIMIT\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIME\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| NOT\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\n\t\t\t| OFFSET\n\t\t\t| ON\n\t\t\t| ONLY\n\t\t\t| OR\n\t\t\t| ORDER\n\t\t\t| PLACING\n\t\t\t| PRIMARY\n\t\t\t| REFERENCES\n\t\t\t| RETURNING\n\t\t\t| SELECT\n\t\t\t| SESSION_USER\n\t\t\t| SOME\n\t\t\t| SYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| SYSTEM_USER\n\t\t\t| TABLE\n\t\t\t| THEN\n\t\t\t| TO\n\t\t\t| TRAILING\n\t\t\t| TRUE_P\n\t\t\t| UNION\n\t\t\t| UNIQUE\n\t\t\t| USER\n\t\t\t| USING\n\t\t\t| VARIADIC\n\t\t\t| WHEN\n\t\t\t| WHERE\n\t\t\t| WINDOW\n\t\t\t| WITH\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * While all keywords can be used as column labels when preceded by AS,\n * not all of them can be used as a \"bare\" column label without AS.\n * Those that can be used as a bare label must be listed here,\n * in addition to appearing in one of the category lists above.\n *\n * Always add a new keyword to this list if possible.  Mark it BARE_LABEL\n * in kwlist.h if it is included here, or AS_LABEL if it is not.\n */\nbare_label_keyword:\n\t\t\t  ABORT_P\n\t\t\t| ABSENT\n\t\t\t| ABSOLUTE_P\n\t\t\t| ACCESS\n\t\t\t| ACTION\n\t\t\t| ADD_P\n\t\t\t| ADMIN\n\t\t\t| AFTER\n\t\t\t| AGGREGATE\n\t\t\t| ALL\n\t\t\t| ALSO\n\t\t\t| ALTER\n\t\t\t| ALWAYS\n\t\t\t| ANALYSE\n\t\t\t| ANALYZE\n\t\t\t| AND\n\t\t\t| ANY\n\t\t\t| ASC\n\t\t\t| ASENSITIVE\n\t\t\t| ASSERTION\n\t\t\t| ASSIGNMENT\n\t\t\t| ASYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| AT\n\t\t\t| ATOMIC\n\t\t\t| ATTACH\n\t\t\t| ATTRIBUTE\n\t\t\t| AUTHORIZATION\n\t\t\t| BACKWARD\n\t\t\t| BEFORE\n\t\t\t| BEGIN_P\n\t\t\t| BETWEEN\n\t\t\t| BIGINT\n\t\t\t| BINARY\n\t\t\t| BIT\n\t\t\t| BOOLEAN_P\n\t\t\t| BOTH\n\t\t\t| BREADTH\n\t\t\t| BY\n\t\t\t| CACHE\n\t\t\t| CALL\n\t\t\t| CALLED\n\t\t\t| CASCADE\n\t\t\t| CASCADED\n\t\t\t| CASE\n\t\t\t| CAST\n\t\t\t| CATALOG_P\n\t\t\t| CHAIN\n\t\t\t| CHARACTERISTICS\n\t\t\t| CHECK\n\t\t\t| CHECKPOINT\n\t\t\t| CLASS\n\t\t\t| CLOSE\n\t\t\t| CLUSTER\n\t\t\t| COALESCE\n\t\t\t| COLLATE\n\t\t\t| COLLATION\n\t\t\t| COLUMN\n\t\t\t| COLUMNS\n\t\t\t| COMMENT\n\t\t\t| COMMENTS\n\t\t\t| COMMIT\n\t\t\t| COMMITTED\n\t\t\t| COMPRESSION\n\t\t\t| CONCURRENTLY\n\t\t\t| CONDITIONAL\n\t\t\t| CONFIGURATION\n\t\t\t| CONFLICT\n\t\t\t| CONNECTION\n\t\t\t| CONSTRAINT\n\t\t\t| CONSTRAINTS\n\t\t\t| CONTENT_P\n\t\t\t| CONTINUE_P\n\t\t\t| CONVERSION_P\n\t\t\t| COPY\n\t\t\t| COST\n\t\t\t| CROSS\n\t\t\t| CSV\n\t\t\t| CUBE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_P\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_CATALOG\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_DATE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_ROLE\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_SCHEMA\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIME\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_USER\n\t\t\t| CURSOR\n\t\t\t| CYCLE\n\t\t\t| DATA_P\n\t\t\t| DATABASE\n\t\t\t| DEALLOCATE\n\t\t\t| DEC\n\t\t\t| DECIMAL_P\n\t\t\t| DECLARE\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT\n\t\t\t| DEFAULTS\n\t\t\t| DEFERRABLE\n\t\t\t| DEFERRED\n\t\t\t| DEFINER\n\t\t\t| DELETE_P\n\t\t\t| DELIMITER\n\t\t\t| DELIMITERS\n\t\t\t| DEPENDS\n\t\t\t| DEPTH\n\t\t\t| DESC\n\t\t\t| DETACH\n\t\t\t| DICTIONARY\n\t\t\t| DISABLE_P\n\t\t\t| DISCARD\n\t\t\t| DISTINCT\n\t\t\t| DO\n\t\t\t| DOCUMENT_P\n\t\t\t| DOMAIN_P\n\t\t\t| DOUBLE_P\n\t\t\t| DROP\n\t\t\t| EACH\n\t\t\t| ELSE\n\t\t\t| EMPTY_P\n\t\t\t| ENABLE_P\n\t\t\t| ENCODING\n\t\t\t| ENCRYPTED\n\t\t\t| END_P\n\t\t\t| ENFORCED\n\t\t\t| ENUM_P\n\t\t\t| ERROR_P\n\t\t\t| ESCAPE\n\t\t\t| EVENT\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDING\n\t\t\t| EXCLUSIVE\n\t\t\t| EXECUTE\n\t\t\t| EXISTS\n\t\t\t| EXPLAIN\n\t\t\t| EXPRESSION\n\t\t\t| EXTENSION\n\t\t\t| EXTERNAL\n\t\t\t| EXTRACT\n\t\t\t| FALSE_P\n\t\t\t| FAMILY\n\t\t\t| FINALIZE\n\t\t\t| FIRST_P\n\t\t\t| FLOAT_P\n\t\t\t| FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t| FORCE\n\t\t\t| FOREIGN\n\t\t\t| FORMAT\n\t\t\t| FORWARD\n\t\t\t| FREEZE\n\t\t\t| FULL\n\t\t\t| FUNCTION\n\t\t\t| FUNCTIONS\n\t\t\t| GENERATED\n\t\t\t| GLOBAL\n\t\t\t| GRANTED\n\t\t\t| GREATEST\n\t\t\t| GROUPING\n\t\t\t| GROUPS\n\t\t\t| HANDLER\n\t\t\t| HEADER_P\n\t\t\t| HOLD\n\t\t\t| IDENTITY_P\n\t\t\t| IF_P\n\t\t\t| ILIKE\n\t\t\t| IMMEDIATE\n\t\t\t| IMMUTABLE\n\t\t\t| IMPLICIT_P\n\t\t\t| IMPORT_P\n\t\t\t| IN_P\n\t\t\t| INCLUDE\n\t\t\t| INCLUDING\n\t\t\t| INCREMENT\n\t\t\t| INDENT\n\t\t\t| INDEX\n\t\t\t| INDEXES\n\t\t\t| INHERIT\n\t\t\t| INHERITS\n\t\t\t| INITIALLY\n\t\t\t| INLINE_P\n\t\t\t| INNER_P\n\t\t\t| INOUT\n\t\t\t| INPUT_P\n\t\t\t| INSENSITIVE\n\t\t\t| INSERT\n\t\t\t| INSTEAD\n\t\t\t| INT_P\n\t\t\t| INTEGER\n\t\t\t| INTERVAL\n\t\t\t| INVOKER\n\t\t\t| IS\n\t\t\t| ISOLATION\n\t\t\t| JOIN\n\t\t\t| JSON\n\t\t\t| JSON_ARRAY\n\t\t\t| JSON_ARRAYAGG\n\t\t\t| JSON_EXISTS\n\t\t\t| JSON_OBJECT\n\t\t\t| JSON_OBJECTAGG\n\t\t\t| JSON_QUERY\n\t\t\t| JSON_SCALAR\n\t\t\t| JSON_SERIALIZE\n\t\t\t| JSON_TABLE\n\t\t\t| JSON_VALUE\n\t\t\t| KEEP\n\t\t\t| KEY\n\t\t\t| KEYS\n\t\t\t| LABEL\n\t\t\t| LANGUAGE\n\t\t\t| LARGE_P\n\t\t\t| LAST_P\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P\n\t\t\t| LEADING\n\t\t\t| LEAKPROOF\n\t\t\t| LEAST\n\t\t\t| LEFT\n\t\t\t| LEVEL\n\t\t\t| LIKE\n\t\t\t| LISTEN\n\t\t\t| LOAD\n\t\t\t| LOCAL\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIME\n\t\t\t| LOCALTIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| LOCATION\n\t\t\t| LOCK_P\n\t\t\t| LOCKED\n\t\t\t| LOGGED\n\t\t\t| MAPPING\n\t\t\t| MATCH\n\t\t\t| MATCHED\n\t\t\t| MATERIALIZED\n\t\t\t| MAXVALUE\n\t\t\t| MERGE\n\t\t\t| MERGE_ACTION\n\t\t\t| METHOD\n\t\t\t| MINVALUE\n\t\t\t| MODE\n\t\t\t| MOVE\n\t\t\t| NAME_P\n\t\t\t| NAMES\n\t\t\t| NATIONAL\n\t\t\t| NATURAL\n\t\t\t| NCHAR\n\t\t\t| NESTED\n\t\t\t| NEW\n\t\t\t| NEXT\n\t\t\t| NFC\n\t\t\t| NFD\n\t\t\t| NFKC\n\t\t\t| NFKD\n\t\t\t| NO\n\t\t\t| NONE\n\t\t\t| NORMALIZE\n\t\t\t| NORMALIZED\n\t\t\t| NOT\n\t\t\t| NOTHING\n\t\t\t| NOTIFY\n\t\t\t| NOWAIT\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\n\t\t\t| NULLIF\n\t\t\t| NULLS_P\n\t\t\t| NUMERIC\n\t\t\t| OBJECT_P\n\t\t\t| OBJECTS_P\n\t\t\t| OF\n\t\t\t| OFF\n\t\t\t| OIDS\n\t\t\t| OLD\n\t\t\t| OMIT\n\t\t\t| ONLY\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR\n\t\t\t| OPTION\n\t\t\t| OPTIONS\n\t\t\t| OR\n\t\t\t| ORDINALITY\n\t\t\t| OTHERS\n\t\t\t| OUT_P\n\t\t\t| OUTER_P\n\t\t\t|
